question: [DIAGRAM: Triangle SQT is right-angled at Q, with point R on SQ such that SR:RQ = 1:3. Triangle QRP is right-angled at Q. PR = ST = 8 cm, QT = 4 cm.] What is the length of PQ, in cm?

steps.title: In right triangle SQT right angle at Q
steps.content: In right triangle SQT (right angle at Q): $ST=8$ (hypotenuse), $QT=4$, so $SQ=\sqrt{8^2-4^2}=\sqrt{48}=4\sqrt{3}$.

steps.title: In right triangle QRP right angle at Q
steps.content: In right triangle QRP (right angle at Q): $PR=8$ (hypotenuse), $RQ=3\sqrt{3}$, so $PQ=\sqrt{64-27}=\sqrt{37}$.

subtopic: Momentum-time graph and Newton's second law
question: [DIAGRAM: graph of momentum (×10^6 kg m s^-1) vs time (s) for an accelerating train, a straight line rising from the origin to about 12×10^6 kg m s^-1 at t = 4 s] A train accelerates from rest along a straight, horizontal track. The force exerted by its motors is constant and there is a constant friction force of $1.8\times10^7$ N. The graph shows how the momentum of the train changes with time. What is the force exerted on the train by its motors?

steps.title: Net force rate of change of momentum gradient
steps.content: Net force = rate of change of momentum = gradient of graph $\approx (12\times10^6)/4 = 3.0\times10^6$ N.

steps.title: Net force motor force friction so motor force
steps.content: Net force = motor force $-$ friction, so motor force $=3.0\times10^6+1.8\times10^7=2.1\times10^7$ N.

question: The curve with equation $y=x^2-4x+5$ meets the straight line with equation $y=2x+c$ at two points, which have x-coordinates $p$ and $q$, where $q>p$. Given that $q-p=8$, what is the value of the constant $c$?

A ship travels into a wave that is travelling in the opposite direction to the ship. The ship has a horizontal speed of 8.0 m s$^{-1}$. The speed of the wave is 3.0 m s$^{-1}$. The front of the ship rises and falls with a time period of 8.0 s. What is the wavelength of the wave?

steps.title: Since ship and wave move toward each other
steps.content: Since ship and wave move toward each other, the relative (encounter) speed is $8.0+3.0=11$ m/s.

steps.title: The s period observed is the encounter period
steps.content: The 8.0 s period observed is the encounter period, so $\lambda = v_{rel}\times T = 11\times8=88$ m.

question: Given that $y=\dfrac{\sin60°-1}{\cos60°}$, what is the value of $y^3$?

subtopic: V-I characteristic of a filament lamp
question: [DIAGRAM: circuit with a 6.0 V battery connected to an 8.0 Ω resistor in series with a filament lamp and an ammeter] The reading on the ammeter is 0.25 A. Which graph is a possible V-I graph for the filament lamp? (Graphs A-F show V (up to 2.0, 4.0, or 6.0 V) plotted against I (0 to 0.25 A), with curves of varying shape.)

steps.title: Voltage across the resistor V
steps.content: Voltage across the 8.0 Ω resistor $=0.25\times8=2.0$ V.

steps.title: The correct graph must pass through with a
steps.content: The correct graph must pass through $(one,V)=(0.25\text{ A}, 4.0\text{ V})$ with a curve typical of a filament lamp (resistance increasing with current).

question: Charlie has a bowl containing red sweets and green sweets only. The sweets are identical in all respects except colour. There are nine sweets in total in the bowl. Charlie eats two sweets from the bowl at random. The probability of Charlie not eating any green sweets is $\frac{5}{12}$. What is the probability that Charlie eats two green sweets?

steps.title: Let number of red sweets so
steps.content: Let $r$ = number of red sweets. $P(\text{no green})=\binom{r}{2}/\binom{9}{2}=5/12$. $\binom{9}{2}=36$, so $\binom{r}{2}=15 \Rightarrow r(r-1)=30 \Rightarrow r=6$.

question: A radioactive nuclide X decays in a single stage to a stable nuclide R. A radioactive nuclide Y decays in a single stage to a stable nuclide S. When a rock formed it contained equal numbers of atoms of all four nuclides X, Y, R and S. The half-life of X is $T$ years and the half-life of Y is $2T$ years. What is the value of $\dfrac{\text{number of atoms of S}}{\text{number of atoms of R}}$ at a time $4T$ years after the rock formed? (Assume no other processes add or remove X, Y, R or S from the rock.)

The greatest diagonal distance between two vertices of a cuboid is $\sqrt{77}$ cm. A similar cuboid has all its lengths exactly half the lengths of the original cuboid. The sides of this smaller cuboid are 2 cm, 3 cm and $x$ cm. What is the value of $x$, in cm?

steps.title: Original cuboid has dimensions double the small one
steps.content: Original cuboid has dimensions double the small one: $4,\,6,\,2x$.

subtopic: Latent heat and specific heat capacity
question: A beaker containing 180 g of water at 25 °C has a 20 g ice cube at 0 °C added to it. No heat is transferred between the water and the surroundings (including the beaker). What is the final temperature of all the water in the beaker after all the ice has melted? (Specific heat capacity of water = 4 J g$^{-1}$ °C$^{-1}$; specific latent heat of fusion of water = 300 J g$^{-1}$.)

steps.title: Heat lost by warm water heat to melt
steps.content: Heat lost by warm water = heat to melt ice + heat to warm melted ice to $T$: $180\times4\times(25-T)=20\times300+20\times4\times T$.

question: A car journey is $m$ miles long. One kilometre is equivalent to $x$ miles. The car uses one litre of fuel to travel a distance of $f$ kilometres. Fuel for the car costs $p$ pence per litre. Which of the following expressions gives the cost of fuel for this journey, in pounds? (There are 100 pence in one pound.)

A pulse of ultrasound travels from one end of a solid uniform rod of length $L$, starting at time $t=0$. The pulse is partially reflected by a crack in the rod and partially by the far end of the rod. These two reflected pulses travel back along the rod, arriving at the end from which they started at times $t_1$ and $t_2$, where $t_2>t_1$. What is the distance between the crack and the far end of the rod?

steps.title: Speed of sound in rod from the far
steps.content: Speed of sound in rod $v=2L/t_2$ (from the far-end echo).

steps.title: Distance to crack
steps.content: Distance to crack $d=v t_1/2 = L t_1/t_2$.

steps.title: Distance from crack to far end
steps.content: Distance from crack to far end $=L-d=L\left(1-\frac{t_1}{t_2}\right)=\frac{(t_2-t_1)}{t_2}L$.

question: Given that $y=\left(2\sqrt{x}-\dfrac{1}{2\sqrt{x}}\right)^2$, find the value of $\dfrac{dy}{dx}$ when $x=\dfrac12$.

subtopic: Newton's second law on inclined and horizontal planes
question: Object P of mass 2.4 kg is on a smooth plane inclined at 60° to the horizontal. A constant force of magnitude $2F$ parallel to the plane is applied to P, so P moves directly up the plane with constant velocity. Object Q of mass 0.75 kg is on a smooth, horizontal plane. A constant force of magnitude $F$ parallel to the plane is applied to Q, so Q moves with constant acceleration. What is the acceleration of Q? (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg$^{-1}$)

steps.title: For P constant velocity N so N
steps.content: For P (constant velocity): $2F=mg\sin60^\circ=2.4\times10\times\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}=12\sqrt{3}$ N, so $F=6\sqrt{3}$ N.

question: A particular arithmetic series has first term $a$ and common difference $d$. The sum of the first $k$ terms of this series is denoted $S_k$. Which of the following is a simplification of $S_{n+1}-S_{n-1}$?

subtopic: Sound wave frequency and displacement from a waveform diagram

What is the frequency of the sound and in which direction has the air at P been displaced from its mean position?

steps.title: From the diagram the cm span corresponds to
steps.content: From the diagram, the 33 cm span corresponds to 2.5 wavelengths, giving $\lambda=33/2.5=13.2$ cm $=0.132$ m.

steps.title: The displacement direction at P is read from
steps.content: The displacement direction at P is read from the diagram's waveform shape (to the left).

subtopic: Solving quadratics in a substituted variable
question: Find how many distinct real solutions there are to the equation $(x^2+4x+3)^2=1$.

[DIAGRAM: circuit with a 30 V dc supply, an 8.0 Ω resistor in series with a parallel combination of a 4.0 Ω heater and a resistor R connected between terminals X and Y] The power transferred in the 4.0 Ω heater is 9.0 W. What is the resistance of R?

steps.title: Voltage across the parallel section V
steps.content: Voltage across the parallel section $V_4=1.5\times4=6$ V.

steps.title: Voltage across the series resistor V giving total
steps.content: Voltage across the 8.0 Ω series resistor $=30-6=24$ V, giving total current $one=24/8=3$ A.

question: The line $x=1$ divides the circle $x^2+y^2=4$ into two segments. What is the area of the smaller segment?

steps.title: Radius chord at so distance from centre
steps.content: Radius $r=2$, chord at $x=1$, so distance from centre $d=1$.

steps.title: Using the reflex angle for the minor segment
steps.content: $\cos(\theta/2)=d/r=1/2 \Rightarrow \theta/2=\pi/3 \Rightarrow \theta=2\pi/3$. using the reflex angle for the minor segment on the far side, $\theta_{major}=2\pi-2\pi/3$; computing directly: minor segment area $=\frac12 r^2(\theta-\sin\theta)$ with $\theta=\frac{4\pi}{3}$ (angle subtended by the minor segment's arc) $=\frac12(4)\left(\frac{4\pi}{3}-\sin\frac{4\pi}{3}\right)=\frac{4\pi}{3}-\sqrt{3}$.

question: [DIAGRAM: uniform plank of length 5.0 m resting horizontally, with a pivot 1.0 m from one end, and a cable at 60° to the horizontal supporting the other end, tension 75 N] What is the weight of the plank?

steps.title: Taking moments about the pivot weight acts at
steps.content: Taking moments about the pivot: weight $W$ acts at the plank's centre, distance $2.5-1.0=1.5$ m from pivot.

steps.title: Cable acts at the far end distance m
steps.content: Cable acts at the far end, distance $5.0-1.0=4.0$ m from pivot, vertical component $=75\sin60^\circ$.

question: What is the mean of $\log_{10}27$, $\log_{10}64$, and $\log_{10}216$?

subtopic: Constant acceleration and equal time intervals
question: [DIAGRAM: straight horizontal road showing four successive oil droplets dripped from an accelerating lorry at constant time interval $t$; the distance between the first two droplets is $x$ and between the final two is $y$] A lorry accelerates along a straight, horizontal road with uniform acceleration. Oil droplets fall from the lorry onto the road at a constant time interval $t$. Which expression gives the acceleration of the lorry?

steps.title: For constant acceleration distances in successive equal time
steps.content: For constant acceleration, distances in successive equal time intervals increase by $at^2$ each interval.

steps.title: With droplets intervals the gap grows from interval
steps.content: With 4 droplets (3 intervals), the gap grows from $x$ (interval 1) to $y=x+2at^2$ (interval 3).

question: Which of the following is the largest in value? (All angles are in radians.)

steps.title: Is decreasing on so among the cosines is
steps.content: $\cos$ is decreasing on $[0,\pi/2]$, so among the cosines $\cos0.5\approx0.878$ is largest. $\sin$ is increasing, so among the sines $\sin1\approx0.841$ is largest.

steps.title: Comparing the two so is overall largest
steps.content: Comparing the two: $\cos0.5>\sin1$, so $\cos0.5$ is overall largest.

question: A geometric progression has first term $u_1=a$ and common ratio $r$. The sum to infinity of the geometric progression is $\frac{8}{5}$. The sum to infinity of the even-numbered terms ($u_2+u_4+u_6+\ldots$) is $\frac{3}{5}$. What is the value of $a+r$?

A child of mass 30 kg is on a sledge of mass 10 kg which is moving down a smooth slope at an instantaneous speed of 4.0 m s$^{-1}$. At this instant, the child jumps backwards off the sledge and lands stationary on the slope. What is the speed of the sledge immediately after the child jumps off?

steps.title: Total initial momentum kg m s
steps.content: Total initial momentum $=(30+10)\times4=160$ kg m s$^{-1}$.

steps.title: After jump child is stationary zero momentum so
steps.content: After jump, child is stationary (zero momentum), so all momentum belongs to the sledge: $10\times v=160 \Rightarrow v=16$ m s$^{-1}$.

steps.title: Volume is conserved halving the diameter quarters the
steps.content: Volume is conserved: halving the diameter quarters the cross-sectional area ($A'=A/4$), so length quadruples ($L'=4L$) to conserve volume.

question: Evaluate $\dfrac{3}{\sqrt{27}+\sqrt{21}} + \dfrac{3}{\sqrt{24}+\sqrt{18}} + \dfrac{3}{\sqrt{21}+\sqrt{15}} + \ldots + \dfrac{3}{\sqrt9+\sqrt3}$

steps.title: Each term sqrt a sqrt b rationalises to
steps.content: Each term 3/(sqrt(a)+sqrt(b)) rationalises to (sqrt(a)-sqrt(b))/2 since a-b=6 throughout.

steps.title: Summing the telescoping series of terms a leaves
steps.content: Summing the telescoping series of 7 terms (a=27,24,.,9) leaves sqrt(27)+sqrt(24)-sqrt(6)-sqrt(3) = 3sqrt(3)+2sqrt(6)-sqrt(6)-sqrt(3)=2sqrt(3)+sqrt(6)=sqrt(3)(2+sqrt(2)).

question: The graph shows potential difference plotted against current for a filament lamp and a resistor. The lamp and resistor are connected in parallel to a 6.0 V power supply and the current in the lamp, $I$, is recorded. subtopic: Transverse wave, phase/displacement at given time
question: A transverse wave on a string has a speed of 500 m/s. The horizontal distance between two points P and Q on the wave, which are one wavelength apart, is 4.0 m. [DIAGRAM: wave snapshot showing points P, X, Q with X at the crest, 6.0 mm amplitude]. At time $t=0$ ms, point X on the string is at its maximum displacement of 6.0 mm above equilibrium. What is the displacement of point X at time $t=7.0$ ms?

options.B: between 0 mm and 6.0 mm above equilibrium
options.D: between 0 mm and 6.0 mm below equilibrium

steps.title: At the phase is of a cycle from
steps.content: At $t=7\,\text{ms}$, the phase is $7/8$ of a cycle $=315^\circ$ from the starting crest.

steps.title: Displacement which lies between and mm above equilibrium
steps.content: Displacement $=A\cos(315^\circ)=6.0\times0.707\approx4.2\,\text{mm}$, which lies between 0 and 6.0 mm above equilibrium.

subtopic: Surface area of cylinder and cube
question: A solid cylinder has radius $r$ cm and height $h$ cm. A cube has side length $3r$ cm. The total surface area of the cylinder is equal to four times the total surface area of the cube. Which of the following is an expression for $h$ in terms of $r$?

steps.title: Setting cylinder SA
steps.content: Setting cylinder SA $=4\times54r^2=216r^2$: $2\pi r^2+2\pi rh=216r^2 \Rightarrow h=\dfrac{216r^2-2\pi r^2}{2\pi r}=r\left(\dfrac{108}{\pi}-1\right)$.

question: A piece of metal of mass 50 g is at thermal equilibrium in a hot liquid at temperature $T$. The metal is removed and immediately placed in 100 g of water at 20°C. The water is stirred and reaches a final temperature of 26°C. Specific heat capacities: hot liquid 2000 J/(kg°C), metal 350 J/(kg°C), water 4200 J/(kg°C). What is the temperature $T$ of the hot liquid? (Assume heat transfers to surroundings are negligible.)

steps.title: Heat lost by metal heat gained by water
steps.content: Heat lost by metal = heat gained by water: $0.05\times350\times(T-26)=0.1\times4200\times6$.

question: The variables $x$ and $y$ are related by the equation $x = 5 - \dfrac{2y^3+1}{1-2y^3}$. Which of the following is a rearrangement to make $y$ the subject?

steps.title: Simplify to the result
steps.content: Multiplying out: $x(1-2y^3)=5(1-2y^3)-(2y^3+1) \Rightarrow x-2xy^3=4-12y^3 \Rightarrow y^3(12-2x)=4-x \Rightarrow y^3=\dfrac{x-4}{2x-12} \Rightarrow y=\sqrt[3]{\dfrac{x-4}{2x-12}}$.

question: A bar magnet is placed at position X close to one end of a coil, on the axis of the coil. [DIAGRAM: coil with bar magnet, velocity-time graph showing the magnet moving from X to Y and back to X]. The graph shows how the velocity of the magnet varies as it moves rapidly to position Y and back to position X. The magnetic field of the bar magnet still affects the coil when at position Y. Which graph represents how the induced voltage in the coil changes as the magnet moves?

options.A: square-wave matching the velocity graph shape exactly (constant positive then constant negative)
options.B: rises from 0 to a peak, dips to 0, rises again to a peak
options.C: single positive rectangular pulse then drops to zero
options.D: starts high positive, decays curving toward zero, small step down to slightly negative, then curves further to more negative by the end
options.E: starts at a peak, descends to a trough near zero, then rises back to a peak (symmetric bowl)
options.F: rises from zero to a positive peak, sharp drop to a negative trough, then curves back toward zero

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: The magnet s field gradient is steepest close
steps.content: The magnet's field gradient is steepest close to the coil (position X) and shallow far away (position Y), so even though velocity is a square wave, induced EMF (which depends on rate of flux change, i.e. field gradient x velocity) decays as the magnet moves away from X to Y, then grows in magnitude (more negative) as it approaches X again on the return: starting high positive, decaying curving toward zero, a small step down to slightly negative, then curving further to more negative by the end.

subtopic: Probability of forming a right-angled triangle
question: Three different numbers are chosen at random from 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. What is the probability that the three numbers form the three sides of a right-angled triangle?

steps.title: There are equally likely triples from
steps.content: There are $\binom{5}{3}=10$ equally likely triples from $\{1,2,3,4,5\}$.

steps.title: A triple forms a right angled triangle exactly
steps.content: A triple forms a right-angled triangle exactly when it is a Pythagorean triple, so test each: only $(3,4,5)$ works, since $3^2+4^2=9+16=25=5^2$.

steps.title: That is one favourable outcome out of ten
steps.content: That is one favourable outcome out of ten, so the probability is $\dfrac{1}{10}$.

The transverse waves creating the stationary wave travel at a speed of 300 m/s through the medium. What is the frequency of the transverse waves?

subtopic: Triangle area with vertex on a circle
question: The diagram shows a semicircle of radius 5 units centred at $(5,0)$, spanning from $(0,0)$ to $(10,0)$. A triangle has vertices at $(0,0)$, $(10,0)$ and $(x,y)$, where $(x,y)$ is a point on the arc of the semicircle. Which of the following is an expression in terms of $x$ for the area of this triangle?

steps.title: Since lies on the circle this gives so
steps.content: Since $(x,y)$ lies on the circle $(x-5)^2+y^2=25$, this gives $y^2 = 25-(x-5)^2=10x-x^2$, so $y=\sqrt{10x-x^2}$.

subtopic: Projectile motion, gravitational field strength comparison
question: A stone is thrown vertically upwards from the surface of the Earth and reaches a maximum height $h$. The same stone is thrown vertically upwards from the surface of the Moon, with the same initial speed. What is the maximum height reached on the Moon? (g on Earth = 10 N/kg; g on Moon = 1.6 N/kg; ignore air resistance.)

steps.title: Using so is inversely proportional to
steps.content: Using $v^2=2gh \Rightarrow h=v^2/(2g)$, so $h$ is inversely proportional to $g$.

question: Four mathematically similar solids, W, X, Y and Z, have the following properties: the ratio of the lengths of W to X is 1:2; the ratio of the surface area of X to Y is 2:1; the ratio of the volume of Y to Z is 1:2. What is the order of the solids when arranged in increasing volume?

steps.title: Length ratio W X gives volume ratio W
steps.content: Length ratio W:X = 1:2 gives volume ratio W:X = $1:8$.

steps.title: Surface area ratio X Y gives length ratio
steps.content: Surface area ratio X:Y = 2:1 gives length ratio X:Y = $\sqrt{2}:1$, so volume ratio X:Y = $2\sqrt{2}:1$.

steps.title: Volume ratio Y Z gives Z
Increasing order W Y Z X i e
steps.content: Increasing order: W(1) < Y(2.83) < Z(5.66) < X(8), i.e.

question: A ray of light is incident on a boundary between a vacuum and medium X at angle $\theta$ to the normal. [DIAGRAM: incident ray, reflected ray, and refracted ray at a vacuum-medium X boundary]. The incident ray is partially reflected and partially refracted. The angle between the reflected and refracted rays is 90°. What is the refractive index of medium X?

steps.title: The reflected ray makes angle with the normal
steps.content: The reflected ray makes angle $\theta$ with the normal (law of reflection).

steps.title: Since the reflected and refracted rays are perpendicular
steps.content: Since the reflected and refracted rays are perpendicular, the refraction angle $r=90^\circ-\theta$.

question: Which one of the following expressions is equal to $\dfrac{(2+\sqrt{20})^2}{(1+\sqrt5)^3}$?

subtopic: Kinematics, force, and power from displacement-time equation
question: An electric train travels along a straight horizontal track, passing point Q at $t=0$. Its distance from Q is given by $x = at + bt^2$, where $a$ and $b$ are constants. Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1: The speed of the train increases with time at a constant rate. 2: The resultant force acting on the train increases with time. 3: The rate at which energy is transferred to the train increases with time.

steps.title: Which increases linearly with time constant rate statement
steps.content: $v=dx/dt=a+2bt$, which increases linearly with time (constant rate) — statement 1 true.

steps.title: Acceleration is constant so resultant force is constant
steps.content: Acceleration $=dv/dt=2b$ is constant, so resultant force $F=m(2b)$ is constant, not increasing — statement 2 false.

steps.title: Power increases with time since increases while is
steps.content: Power $=Fv=2mb(a+2bt)$ increases with time since $v$ increases while $F$ is constant — statement 3 true.

question: Evaluate $\displaystyle\sum_{r=2}^{\infty}\left(\dfrac{1}{2^r}+\dfrac{1}{3^r}+\dfrac{1}{4^r}\right)$.

steps.title: Each is a geometric series starting at
steps.content: Each is a geometric series starting at $r=2$: $\sum_{r=2}^\infty \frac{1}{k^r} = \dfrac{1/k^2}{1-1/k}$.

subtopic: Resistance and resistivity with dimension scaling, parallel combination
question: A length of wire has resistance $R$. Another length of wire is made from the same material, twice as long, with half the diameter. Both wires have circular cross-sections. The two wires are connected in parallel. What is the total resistance of this combination?

steps.title: The second wire has and half diameter quarter
steps.content: The second wire has $L_2=2L$ and $A_2=A/4$ (half diameter $\Rightarrow$ quarter area), so $R_2=\rho(2L)/(A/4)=8\rho L/A=8R$.

question: What is the complete set of real values of $x$ for which $x^2(x^2+4) < 21$?

subtopic: Work, efficiency, lifting water from a well
question: An empty bucket has a mass of 1.20 kg and internal volume of $0.0150\,\text{m}^3$. The bucket is used to lift water from a well, attached to a rope winding onto a rotating cylinder of radius 0.200 m turned by a handle. To lift a bucket completely full of water at constant speed, a force of 250 N must be applied to the handle, acting tangent to the circle of radius 0.600 m in which the handle moves. The energy required to lift the (empty) bucket is wasted energy. What is the efficiency of the system in lifting water from the well? (g = 10 N/kg; density of water = 1000 kg/m³.)

steps.title: Per revolution input work and output useful work
steps.content: Per revolution, input work $\propto 250\times0.6$ and output (useful) work $\propto 150\times0.2$ (same rope displacement factor of $2\pi$ cancels).

question: A sector of a circle has perimeter 24. For what value of the radius does the sector have the maximum possible area?

A uniform metre ruler has a mass of 100 g and is 1.00 m long. A small object of mass 20 g is fixed at the 0 cm mark, and another of mass 80 g at the 100 cm mark. The ruler is balanced on a pivot. When balanced, what distance is the pivot from the 0 cm mark?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: Taking moments about the pivot at distance from
steps.content: Taking moments about the pivot at distance $d$ from the 0 cm mark, with the ruler's own mass acting at 50 cm: $20(0)+100(50)+80(100)=200d \Rightarrow d=\dfrac{5000+8000}{200}=65\,\text{cm}$.

subtopic: Quadratic curve, rectangle from roots and turning point
question: The curve $y=x^2-x-6$ intersects the x-axis at points A and B, and has a minimum at point C. The rectangle ABDE has two of its vertices at A and B, and C lies on edge DE, between D and E. What is the area of the rectangle ABDE?

steps.title: The minimum is at so the vertical distance
steps.content: The minimum is at $x=\tfrac12$: $y=\tfrac14-\tfrac12-6=-6.25$, so the vertical distance from AB to the minimum (and hence to DE) is $6.25$.

subtopic: Kirchhoff's laws, circuit with two batteries
question: A circuit comprising two resistors (3.0 Ω and 1.0 Ω) and two batteries (3.0 V and 6.0 V), with negligible internal resistance, is set up as shown, with junctions X and Y. [DIAGRAM: circuit with 3.0 Ω and 1.0 Ω resistors and 3.0 V, 6.0 V batteries between junctions X and Y]. What are the magnitude and direction of the current in the 3.0 V battery?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: Applying Kirchhoff s voltage and current laws to
steps.content: Applying Kirchhoff's voltage and current laws to the two-loop circuit with the given branch resistances and EMFs yields a current of 2.0 A in the 3.0 V battery branch, directed from Y to X.

subtopic: Perpendicular distance from a point to a line
question: A straight line passes through the points $(0, 2a)$ and $(a, 0)$, where $a$ is a positive constant. What is the perpendicular distance of the point $P(a, 2a)$ from this line?

steps.title: The line through and has equation slope
steps.content: The line through $(0,2a)$ and $(a,0)$ has equation $2x+y-2a=0$ (slope $-2$).

subtopic: Perfectly inelastic collision, kinetic energy loss
question: Two isolated spheres have masses $m$ and $2m$. They move towards each other along the same line with speeds $4v$ and $v$ respectively, then collide and coalesce. What is the loss of kinetic energy during the collision?

steps.title: Taking the direction of mass as positive momentum
steps.content: Taking the direction of mass $m$ as positive, momentum conservation: $m(4v)+2m(-v)=3m\,v_f \Rightarrow v_f=\dfrac{2v}{3}$.

question: Find the sum of the real solutions to the equation $2^x - \left(\sqrt2\right)^{x+6} + 12 = 0$.

subtopic: Young modulus, extension of a hanging cable under its own weight
question: A steel cable has mass 64 kg and cross-sectional area $2.0\times10^{-4}\,\text{m}^2$. The Young modulus of steel is $2.0\times10^{11}\,\text{Pa}$. When the cable lies on horizontal ground its length is 40 m. What is its extension when it is suspended freely from one end and hangs vertically? (g = 10 N/kg; assume the cable obeys Hooke's law.)

steps.title: For a cable hanging under its own weight
steps.content: For a cable hanging under its own weight, the tension varies linearly from 0 at the bottom to $W=mg$ at the top, so the effective average force is $W/2$.

subtopic: surface area of sphere vs cylinders
question: The surface area of a solid sphere of radius R is equal to the total surface area of 10 solid closed cylinders of radius r and height 4r. Which of the following is an expression for R in terms of r? (The surface area of a sphere of radius $R$ is $4\pi R^2$.)

steps.title: Each cylinder s total surface area
steps.content: Each cylinder's total surface area = $2\pi r^2 + 2\pi r(4r) = 10\pi r^2$.

steps.title: Set equal to sphere
steps.content: Set equal to sphere: $4\pi R^2 = 100\pi r^2 \Rightarrow R^2 = 25r^2 \Rightarrow R = 5r$.

question: Which of the following is a correct rearrangement of $y = p - \dfrac{q-r}{s-x}$ to make x the subject?

A spaceship of mass 10000 kg is moving at 2.0 m/s relative to a space station. The spaceship is captured by a robotic arm attached to the space station and brought to rest by a force of 1000 N. How far will the spaceship move in its initial direction relative to the space station while the force is being applied? (Assume that the acceleration of the space station is negligible.)

A circuit has a battery, an ammeter, and three identical resistors. [DIAGRAM: battery in series with one resistor, then two further identical resistors arranged so one branch contains a switch; ammeter reads total circuit current]. When the switch is open, the reading on the ammeter is 1.0 A and the power transferred from the battery is 1.0 W. The switch is now closed. What is the new reading on the ammeter and what is the new power transferred from the battery?

steps.title: With switch open current flows through resistors in
steps.content: With switch open, current flows through 2 resistors in series (3rd branch open): total resistance $2R$, $one=1.0$A so EMF $= 2R$, and $P=one^2(2R)=1.0$W gives $2R=1\,\Omega$ so $R=0.5\,\Omega$, EMF $=1.0$V.

steps.title: Closing the switch puts the third identical resistor
steps.content: Closing the switch puts the third identical resistor in parallel with one of the existing resistors, giving total resistance $R + R/2 = 1.5R = 0.75\,\Omega$.

steps.title: Total current A power W W
The ammeter in the branch shared by both
steps.content: The ammeter (in the branch shared by both resistors, not the parallel pair) reads half of this branch current: $1.33/2\approx0.67$A.

subtopic: area of a triangle inside a square using ratios
question: WXYZ is a square of side length 1. Points M, N, P lie on sides WX, XY, YZ respectively (in that cyclic order around the square), with WM:MX = 1:2, XN:NY = 3:1, YP:PZ = 4:1. [DIAGRAM: square WXYZ with points M, N, P marked on three sides as described]. What is the area of triangle MNP?

subtopic: elastic potential energy graph, spring constant
question: A spring is initially unstretched. A force F is used to stretch the spring. The extension x and the energy E stored in the stretched spring are measured for different values of F. The graph shows how the energy E, in J, varies with the extension squared, $x^2$, in cm$^2$: a straight line through the origin passing through the point $(25, 0.015)$. What is the magnitude of F when the spring stores 0.015 J of energy?

steps.title: For a spring so the slope of E
steps.content: For a spring, $E = \tfrac{1}{2}kx^2$, so the slope of E vs $x^2$ is $k/2$.

steps.title: At this point m so N
steps.content: At this point $x = 0.05$ m, so $F = kx = 12 \times 0.05 = 0.60$ N.

question: Given that $\dfrac{27^{2(x-2)}}{9^{(2x-3)}} = (81)^{3/2}$, what is the value of $x$?

steps.title: Write everything as powers of and

question: A solid, cylindrical metal bar has a uniform cross-sectional area of 12 cm$^2$ and a volume of 180 cm$^3$. The bar rests on a horizontal surface on one of its circular faces. The pressure on the surface due to the bar is 0.45 N/cm$^2$. What is the density of the metal, in g/cm$^3$? (gravitational field strength = 10 N/kg)

steps.title: Weight N so mass kg g
steps.content: Weight $= P \times A = 0.45 \times 12 = 5.4$ N, so mass $= 5.4/10 = 0.54$ kg $= 540$ g.

question: Last year, the salary of the coach of a football club was 80% of the salary of the star player. At the start of the new year, the coach received a 15% increase in salary and the star player received a 38% increase in salary. What percentage of the star player's new salary is the coach's new salary?

Two samples of pure radioactive isotopes X and Y decay with half-lives of 2 days and 3 days, respectively. Both X and Y decay in a single step into different stable isotopes. Initially the number of atoms of X is twice the number of atoms of Y. After how many days are the expected numbers of atoms of X and Y equal to each other?

options.A: The expected numbers of atoms of X and Y are never equal.

question: An athlete's training session consists of several complete repetitions of a three-part programme: 1. Walk 100 m at an average speed of 6 km/h. 2. Jog 200 m at an average speed of 10 km/h. 3. Run 100 m at an average speed of 20 km/h. What is the athlete's average speed for the complete training session, in km/h?

steps.title: Using a common denominator of h
steps.content: Using a common denominator of 600: $10/600+12/600+3/600 = 25/600 = 1/24$ h.

subtopic: steady-state temperature gradient through two materials
question: A large, flat, metal plate is coated on one side with a layer of thermally insulating material of the same thickness a as the metal plate. The uninsulated top surface of the metal plate is maintained at a constant temperature $T_1$. The bottom surface of the insulating material is maintained at a constant, lower temperature $T_2$. The system is in equilibrium. [DIAGRAM: metal plate (thickness a) on top of insulating layer (thickness a); temperature varies from $T_1$ at top to $T_2$ at bottom]. Which graph could show how the temperature varies with distance from the top surface of the metal plate to the bottom surface of the insulating material?

options.A: [DIAGRAM: graph showing a very shallow/near-flat temperature drop across the metal (0 to a, good conductor) then a steep near-linear drop across the insulator (a to 2a)]
options.B: [DIAGRAM: graph showing a steep drop across the metal, then shallow drop across the insulator]
options.C: [DIAGRAM: graph showing linear uniform drop across the entire distance 0 to 2a]
options.D: [DIAGRAM: graph showing no change across metal, sudden step change at a, then flat]
options.E: [DIAGRAM: graph showing curved (non-linear) drop across both sections]

steps.title: In steady state the same rate of heat
steps.content: In steady state, the same rate of heat flow passes through both layers (same thickness a, same area).

steps.title: Metal is a good conductor low thermal resistance
steps.content: Metal is a good conductor (low thermal resistance) so very little temperature drop occurs across it; the insulator has high thermal resistance so almost all the temperature drop $(T_1-T_2)$ occurs across it, roughly linearly since it's a uniform material at steady state.

steps.title: This matches graph A near flat over the
steps.content: This matches graph A: near-flat over the metal (0 to a), then a steep near-linear decrease over the insulator (a to 2a).

subtopic: similar solids, surface area and volume ratios
question: Two objects X and Y are similar. The surface area of object Y is double the surface area of object X. The volume of object Y is $7\sqrt{2}$ cm$^3$ more than the volume of object X. What is the volume of object X, in cm$^3$?

steps.title: Linear scale factor satisfies so and volume ratio
steps.content: Linear scale factor $k$ satisfies $k^2=2$ so $k=\sqrt{2}$, and volume ratio $k^3=2\sqrt{2}$.

steps.title: Let be X s volume V X V
steps.content: Let $V_X$ be X's volume: $2\sqrt{2}\,V_X - V_X = 7\sqrt{2} \Rightarrow V_X(2\sqrt{2}-1)=7\sqrt{2} \Rightarrow V_X = \dfrac{7\sqrt{2}}{2\sqrt{2}-1}

question: The voltage output of a power station is stepped up using a transformer before the power is transmitted to a distant town. The primary coil of this transformer has 300 turns and the secondary coil has 1500 turns. In the town, a step-down transformer reduces the voltage supplied by the transmission cables to 33 000 V for distribution within the town. The step-down transformer supplies a current of 1500 A. The current in the transmission cables is 450 A and both transformers are ideal and 100% efficient. What is the voltage output of the power station? (Assume that the resistance of the transmission cables is negligible.)

steps.title: This equals the power on the transmission line
steps.content: This equals the power on the transmission line, so line voltage $= P/450 = 110000$ V.

steps.title: This is the secondary voltage of the step
steps.content: This is the secondary voltage of the step-up transformer, which steps up by a factor $1500/300=5$.

question:
The equation
$\left(\dfrac{a\times10^4+2a\times10^3}{3\times10^{-1}}\right)^2=8\times10^9$
has two solutions for $a$. What is the positive difference between these two solutions?

steps.title: The positive difference between the two solutions is
steps.content: The positive difference between the two solutions is $2\sqrt{5}$.

subtopic: transverse wave, average particle speed over integer cycles
question: A transverse wave with an amplitude of 3.0 cm travels along a stretched string. The wave has a frequency of 12 Hz and a wavelength of 0.25 m. What is the average speed of a particle in the string as the string oscillates during a time of 2.0 s?

steps.title: In s there are exactly complete cycles
steps.content: In 2.0 s there are exactly $2.0/T = 24$ complete cycles.

steps.title: Each cycle a particle travels a total path
steps.content: Each cycle a particle travels a total path of $4A = 4\times3.0=12$ cm (up to crest, back through zero to trough, back to zero).

steps.title: Total distance cm over s giving average speed
steps.content: Total distance $=24\times12=288$ cm over 2.0 s, giving average speed $=288/2.0=144$ cm/s.

subtopic: vectors, ratio division of a line segment
question: X and Y are the end-points of a line segment. Point P has coordinates $(-8, 5)$. P lies on the line segment XY such that XP:PY is 1:2 and $\overrightarrow{XP} = \begin{pmatrix}4\\-3\end{pmatrix}$. A point Q is such that $\overrightarrow{QY} = \begin{pmatrix}7\\6\end{pmatrix}$. What are the coordinates of point Q?

A battery and two resistors X and Y are connected in series. The power transferred by the battery is 6 W. The resistance of X is 10 $\Omega$. The voltage across Y is 4 V. What is the current in the circuit?

steps.title: Factoring so A taking the positive root
steps.content: Factoring: $(5I-3)(one+1)=0$, so $one = 3/5$ A (taking the positive root).

question: Find the maximum value of $2^{\sin x} \times 3^{-\sin x}$ where $0° \le x \le 360°$.

steps.title: Since this is maximised when is as small
steps.content: Since $\dfrac23<1$, this is maximised when $\sin x$ is as small as possible, i.e. $\sin x=-1$ (at $x=270^\circ$).

question: A diver at the bottom of a lake of depth d fills a syringe with an ideal gas and seals the nozzle. The piston remains free to move. The volume of the gas in the syringe at the bottom of the lake is 90 cm$^3$. As the diver returns to the surface, the temperature of the gas does not change. At the surface of the lake the gas in the syringe is at atmospheric pressure and the volume of the gas is 720 cm$^3$. What is the volume of the gas in the syringe at a depth $d/2$?

steps.title: Since pressure increases linearly with depth
steps.content: Since pressure increases linearly with depth, $P_{bottom}=P_{atm}+\rho g d = 8P_{atm} \Rightarrow \rho g d = 7P_{atm}$.

question: $x^2-x-6$ is a factor of $x^3+ax^2+2x+b$, where a and b are real constants. What is the value of $a+b$?

Two pipes contain air at the same temperature and pressure. A stationary sound wave is formed in the first pipe, which is closed at one end and open at the other end. The lowest frequency of stationary sound wave that can be formed in this pipe is 4000 Hz. The second pipe has the same length as the first pipe, but is open at both ends. What is the lowest frequency of stationary sound wave that can be formed in the second pipe?

steps.title: Closed pipe fundamental Hz so
steps.content: Closed pipe fundamental: $f_1 = v/4L = 4000$ Hz, so $L=v/16000$.

steps.title: Open pipe fundamental Hz
steps.content: Open pipe fundamental: $f = v/2L = v/(2\times v/16000) = 8000$ Hz.

question: An arithmetic progression has first term a and common difference d. The sum of the first 9 terms plus the sum of the first 10 terms is equal to the sum of the first 11 terms. Which of the following is a correct expression for a in terms of d?

A cannon ball of mass 2.6 kg is fired horizontally from a cannon on the top of a cliff at a speed of 90 m/s. The height of the cliff above the horizontal ground below is 45 m. What is the magnitude of the impulse that acts on the ball between leaving the cannon and reaching the ground? (gravitational field strength = 10 N/kg; assume that air resistance is negligible)

steps.title: Vertical velocity gained m s horizontal velocity is
steps.content: Vertical velocity gained: $v_y=gt=30$ m/s (horizontal velocity is unchanged, so impulse is purely due to the vertical velocity change).

question: The first three terms of a convergent geometric progression are: $2p$, $p-3$, $p-7$. What is the sum to infinity of this progression?

steps.title: For terms are ratio convergent first term sum
steps.content: For $p=9$: terms are $18, 6, 2$, ratio $r=1/3$ (convergent), first term $a=18$, sum to infinity $=18/(1-1/3)=27$.

steps.title: For terms are ratio divergent rejected

subtopic: friction on an incline, power dissipated
question: A child on a sledge is travelling straight down a snow-covered slope at a constant speed of 15 m/s. The mass of the child and sledge together is 60 kg. The angle of the slope to the horizontal is 30°. What is the rate at which thermal energy is being produced due to friction forces? (gravitational field strength = 10 N/kg)

steps.title: Constant speed means friction balances the component of
steps.content: Constant speed means friction balances the component of gravity along the slope: $f = mg\sin\theta = 60\times10\times\sin30^\circ

steps.title: Rate of thermal energy production W
steps.content: Rate of thermal energy production $= f\times v = 300\times15=4500$ W.

question: The diagram shows a circle with centre O, with an angle of 40° and an angle of 55° marked, and x is the angle to be found. [DIAGRAM: circle with centre O; two chords/radii forming angles 40° and 55° with an inscribed or central angle x]. What is the value of x?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: Using circle theorems angles subtended by the same
steps.content: Using circle theorems (angles subtended by the same arc, and the fact that the angle at the centre is twice the angle at the circumference, together with the given 40^\circ and 55^\circ), the value of x that satisfies the geometric constraints in the diagram is 75^\circ.

question: The diagram shows a battery with no internal resistance and four identical resistors P, Q, R and S. [DIAGRAM: resistor network with P, Q, R, S arranged in a combination of series/parallel branches connected to the battery]. Resistor Q dissipates 4.0 W of power. What is the total power supplied by the battery?

steps.title: Read the network topology
steps.content: The four identical resistors of resistance $R$ form the chain $P$ in series with the parallel pair $Q\parallel R$, then $S$ in series. The equivalent resistance is $$R_{\mathrm{eq}} = R + \dfrac{R}{2} + R = \dfrac{5}{2}R.$$

steps.title: Relate battery current to power in Q
steps.content: Battery current $I$ splits equally through $Q$ and $R$, so each parallel branch carries $\dfrac{I}{2}$. Power in $Q$ is $$\left(\dfrac{I}{2}\right)^2 R = \dfrac{I^2 R}{4} = 4.0\,\mathrm{W} \implies I^2 R = 16\,\mathrm{W}.$$

question: Find the real value of $x$ that satisfies $\log_3(x^2+3x+2)=2+\log_3(x^2+2x)$.

steps.title: Combine logs log x x x x so
This gives x x discriminant x so x
Checking domain both log arguments must be positive
steps.content: Checking domain (both log arguments must be positive): x=-2 gives x^2+2x=0, undefined; x=1/8 gives both arguments positive.

question: Two identical, uniform, thin planks, each of mass 40 kg, are propped up against one another in equilibrium on a rough surface, forming an A-frame shape. Each plank is at an angle of 30° to the vertical. [DIAGRAM: two planks leaning against each other at their tops, each at 30° to vertical, with point P marked at the base of one plank on the ground]. What is the magnitude of the friction force at point P? (gravitational field strength = 10 N/kg)

steps.title: Set up forces on one plank
steps.content: Each plank has weight $W = 40 \times 10 = 400\,\mathrm{N}$ and makes $30^\circ$ with the vertical. By symmetry the apex reaction is horizontal, so the normal reaction at the base equals $W$. Friction $f$ at the base balances that horizontal apex force.

steps.title: Take moments about the apex
steps.content: With plank length $L$, moments about the apex give $$W \cdot \dfrac{L}{2}\sin 30^\circ = f \cdot L\cos 30^\circ.$$

subtopic: definite integral involving a square root
question: Which of the following is equal to $\displaystyle\int_{-(3-\sqrt5)}^{3-\sqrt5} \frac{x^2}{3-\sqrt5}\,dx$?

subtopic: Newton's third law, blocks in contact
question: Three blocks P, Q and R are in contact on a horizontal surface, in the order P-Q-R, with a horizontal force applied to P. The mass of P is 4.0 kg and the masses of Q and R are each 2.0 kg. A horizontal force of 20 N is applied to P so that all three blocks move together. The friction force between P and the surface is 2.0 N, and the friction forces between Q and R and the surface are each 1.0 N. What is the magnitude of the force that R exerts on Q?

steps.title: Total mass kg total friction N
steps.content: Total mass $=8.0$ kg, total friction $=2.0+1.0+1.0=4.0$ N.

steps.title: Consider block R alone the only horizontal forces
steps.content: Consider block R alone: the only horizontal forces are the push from Q, $N_{QR}$, and friction $1.0$ N opposing motion.

steps.title: Newton s second law N
steps.content: Newton's second law: $N_{QR}-1.0 = m_R a = 2.0\times2.0=4.0 \Rightarrow N_{QR}=5.0$ N.

steps.title: By Newton s third law the force R
steps.content: By Newton's third law, the force R exerts on Q has the same magnitude, 5.0 N.

subtopic: first and second derivatives, simultaneous equations
question: A function f is defined by $f(x) = \dfrac{a}{x}+\dfrac{b}{x^2}$ where a and b are constants. It is given that $f'(1) = 2$ and $f''(-1) = -2$. What is the value of $a+b$?

steps.title: F x a x b x f a
Solving and subtracting gives then
steps.content: Solving $-a-2b=2$ and $-a+3b=-1$: subtracting gives $5b=-3 \Rightarrow b=-3/5$, then $-a = 2+2(-3/5) = 2-6/5=4/5 \Rightarrow a=-4/5$.

question:
An object is released from rest at a height $H$ above the ground and falls freely in a uniform gravitational field.

At time $t$ after being released, it has fallen a distance $s$ and is at a height $h$ above the ground, travelling at speed $v$.

The graph shows two quantities plotted.

| Row | y-axis | x-axis |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | $h$ | $t$ |
| 2 | kinetic energy | $h$ |
| 3 | gravitational potential energy | $s$ |

Which of the rows show(s) a possible pair of quantities for the axes?

(Assume that air resistance is negligible.)

steps.title: Read the given graph
steps.content: The plotted figure is a straight line of negative gradient with a positive $y$-intercept and a positive $x$-intercept.

steps.title: Reject height against time
steps.content: Row 1 plots $h$ against $t$, so $h=H-\tfrac{1}{2}gt^{2}$. That is a downward-opening parabola in $t$, not a straight line.

steps.title: Match kinetic energy to height
steps.content: Row 2: lost gravitational energy appears as kinetic energy, $E_{k}=mg(H-h)$. This decreases linearly in $h$, from $mgH$ at $h=0$ to $0$ at $h=H$.

steps.title: Match potential to distance fallen
steps.content: Row 3: $E_{p}=mg(H-s)$ from the ground: the same linear decreasing form in $s$. Rows 2 and 3 both match the figure.

solution.fast: Rows 2 and 3 are the same energy trade read from opposite ends of $H$, so they stand or fall together; only those two are linear.
solution.trap: Choosing the $h$ against $t$ pairing treats a quadratic free-fall graph as if it were a straight line.

question: Find the real value of x that satisfies $\log_3(x^2+3x+2) = 2+\log_3(x^2+2x)$.

steps.title: Checking domain arguments of logs must be positive
steps.content: Checking domain (arguments of logs must be positive): $x=-2$ makes $x^2+2x=0$ (invalid), so $x=1/8$.

subtopic: resistivity, resistance after re-forming a conductor
question: A 20 V battery of negligible internal resistance is connected in series with a 20 $\Omega$ resistor and a cylindrical conductor. The current in the circuit is 0.20 A. The cylindrical conductor is now removed, melted and re-formed into a new cylinder. After the cylinder has cooled to its original temperature, its length is 4 times greater than that of the original cylinder. What is the resistance of the new cylinder?

steps.title: When re formed with the same volume but
steps.content: When re-formed with the same volume but 4x the length, the cross-sectional area becomes $A/4$.

subtopic: interference via reflection with phase change
question: A sound wave can travel from a source at P to a detector at R directly or by reflecting at Q. The angle between PR and PQ is 30° and the distance from P to R is d. [DIAGRAM: source P, detector R, reflecting surface with reflection point Q; angle QPR = 30°, distance PR = d]. There is a phase difference of $\pi$ radians between the incident and reflected wave at Q. Waves that reach R via Q are in phase with waves that reach R directly from P. Which expression gives the greatest wavelength of sound waves for which this is true?

steps.title: Find the geometric path difference
steps.content: Triangle $PQR$ is isosceles with $\angle QPR = \angle QRP = 30^\circ$, so $$PQ = QR = \dfrac{d/2}{\cos 30^\circ} = \dfrac{d}{\sqrt{3}}.$$ The path difference is $$\delta = PQ + QR - PR = \dfrac{2d}{\sqrt{3}} - d = d\left(\dfrac{2}{\sqrt{3}} - 1\right).$$

steps.title: Include the reflection phase shift
steps.content: A phase change of $\pi$ at $Q$ is equivalent to an extra path of $\dfrac{\lambda}{2}$. For the two arrivals at the detector to be in phase, $$\delta + \dfrac{\lambda}{2} = n\lambda \quad (n = 1, 2, 3, \ldots).$$

steps.title: Maximise the wavelength
steps.content: Rearranging, $\lambda = \dfrac{\delta}{n - 1/2}$. The greatest wavelength is at $n = 1$: $$\lambda = 2\delta = 2d\left(\dfrac{2}{\sqrt{3}} - 1\right).$$

subtopic: maximum value using auxiliary angle / Cauchy-Schwarz on a circle
question: Given that $x^2+y^2=1$, what is the greatest possible value of $2x+3y$?

steps.title: By the Cauchy Schwarz inequality or writing the
steps.content: By the Cauchy-Schwarz inequality (or writing $x=\cos\theta,y=\sin\theta$), the maximum of $ax+by$ subject to $x^2+y^2=1$ is $\sqrt{a^2+b^2}$.

question: Two springs have spring constants of 200 N/m and 600 N/m, respectively. The springs are joined in series, end-to-end, and stretched so that their combined extension is 0.80 m. What is the total strain energy stored in the springs?

steps.title: For springs in series the same force F
steps.content: For springs in series, the same force F acts through both, and total extension $x_{total} = F/k_1+F/k_2 = F\left(\dfrac{1}{200}+\dfrac{1}{600}\right)=F\times\dfrac{4}{600}=\dfrac{F}{150}$.

steps.title: Total strain energy J
steps.content: Total strain energy $=\tfrac12 F x_{total} = \tfrac12\times120\times0.80=48$ J.

question: During summer activities week 120 students each chose one activity from swimming, archery, and tennis. 46 of the students were girls. 36 of the students chose tennis, and $\frac23$ of these were boys; 25 girls chose swimming, and 27 students chose archery. A boy is picked at random. What is the probability that he chose swimming?

steps.title: Boys in tennis girls in tennis so girls
steps.content: Boys in tennis $=24$, girls in tennis $=12$, so girls in archery $=46-12-25=9$, boys in archery $=27-9=18$.

steps.content: Rewrite 9 and 27 as powers of 3 (9=3^2, 27=3^3) and combine exponents using the index laws to reach a single power of 3, giving 3^(4n).

question: PQRS is part of a regular polygon with n sides. The side PQ is extended to T such that PQT is a straight line. The length of RQ is the same as the length of RT. [DIAGRAM: regular polygon fragment PQRS with PQ extended to T, RQ=RT, angle QRT=x marked at R] Find an equation for n in terms of x, where x is the size of angle ∠QRT in degrees.

steps.title: Each interior angle is so the exterior angle
steps.content: Each interior angle is $\frac{(n-2)180}{n}$, so the exterior angle $\angle RQT=\frac{360}{n}$.

steps.title: Since triangle is isosceles with base angles so
steps.content: Since $RQ=RT$, triangle $RQT$ is isosceles with base angles $\frac{360}{n}$, so $x=180-\frac{720}{n}$, giving $n=\dfrac{720}{180-x}$.

steps.content: Gamma rays have very short wavelength/high frequency; microwaves have long wavelength/low frequency.

steps.title: The shift from gamma to microwave means wavelength
steps.content: The shift from gamma to microwave means wavelength increased and frequency decreased.

question: A nuclide $^{214}_{82}$Pb changes by radioactive decay into the nuclide $^{210}_{82}$Pb. Which combination of emissions produces this change?

steps.title: Mass number drops by needs exactly alpha A

steps.title: Atomic number stays the same alpha alone gives
steps.content: Atomic number stays the same (82→82): 1 alpha alone gives ΔZ=-2, so 2 beta emissions (ΔZ=+1 each) are needed to restore Z. 1 alpha + 2 beta.

question: A sample of radioactive isotope X decays to isotope Y. Y has a half-life double that of X. Initially only X is present. [DIAGRAM: five candidate graphs of number of nuclei of X and Y against time] Which graph could represent how the numbers of nuclei of X and Y vary with time?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: X decays away with its own shorter half
steps.content: X decays away with its own (shorter) half-life while Y is produced from X's decay and then decays more slowly (longer half-life), so Y's population rises, peaks, then falls more gradually than X's — matching graph A.

question: Bronze is a mixture of tin and copper. A sample contains 10% tin by volume. What percentage of the mass of the sample is tin? (Density of tin = Y, density of copper = X.)

steps.title: Mass fraction of tin mass of tin total
steps.content: Mass fraction of tin = (mass of tin)/(total mass) = 0.1Y/(0.1Y+0.9X).

steps.title: Multiplying numerator and denominator by gives Y Y
steps.content: Multiplying numerator and denominator by 10 gives Y/(Y+9X) = Y/(9X+Y), so the percentage by mass is Y/(9X+Y) x 100.

question: When a stationary uranium-238 nucleus decays by alpha emission it forms a thorium-234 nucleus. The total kinetic energy produced is E. What is the kinetic energy of the alpha particle?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: By momentum conservation the lighter alpha particle carries
steps.content: By momentum conservation the lighter alpha particle carries most of the energy: $KE_\alpha=E\times\frac{m_{Th}}{m_{Th}+m_\alpha}=\frac{234}{238}E$.

question: A loudspeaker emitting regular clicks sits between two buildings 128m apart; the student and loudspeaker are 48m from one building. Each click produces two echoes. The click rate is increased until each echo coincides with a new click. What is the frequency of emission when this happens? (Speed of sound = 320 m/s.)

steps.title: Echo delays near building s far building s
steps.content: Echo delays: near building $2\times48/320=0.3$s, far building $2\times80/320=0.5$s.

steps.title: The click period must be the highest common
steps.content: The click period must be the highest common factor of these: $T=0.1$s, so $f=1/T=10$Hz.

question: Which one of the following atoms or ions contains the same number of neutrons and electrons as $^{40}_{20}$Ca²⁺?

steps.content: Ca^2⁺ has 20 neutrons and 18 electrons (20 protons - 2).

steps.title: K has neutrons and electrons matches
steps.content: K⁺-39 has $39-19=20$ neutrons and $19-1=18$ electrons — matches.

question: Solid titanium oxide does not conduct electricity and cannot be electrolysed. When molten it conducts and can be electrolysed. 7.2g of titanium form for every 3.6dm³ of oxygen at rtp. Which statements, if any, are correct? 1: after electrolysis the titanium has a noble gas electron configuration. 2: when molten, delocalised electrons carry the charge. 3: the empirical formula of titanium oxide is TiO2. steps.title: Moles Ti moles O so moles O atoms
steps.content: Moles Ti $=7.2/48=0.15$; moles O2 $=3.6/24=0.15$, so moles O atoms $=0.30$.

steps.title: Ti O formula TiO statement true
Statement is false Ti Ti ions don t
steps.content: Statement 1 is false (Ti^3⁺/Ti⁴⁺ ions don't have noble gas configuration); statement 2 describes ionic melts, not delocalised electrons, so false.

question: In which, if any, of the following reactions are covalent bonds both broken and formed? 1: burning sodium in oxygen. 2: electrolysis of aqueous sodium chloride. 3: displacement of iron from iron oxide by heating with aluminium powder.

steps.content: Electrolysis of aqueous NaCl breaks covalent bonds in water and forms new covalent bonds (H2, O2, Cl2 are covalent molecules).

steps.content: Burning sodium and the thermite reaction mainly involve ionic compounds.

question: In a reversible reaction, gaseous P and Q form gaseous R and S. Increasing temperature increases both rate and equilibrium yield. Increasing pressure increases rate but yield is unaffected. Which equation could represent the reaction?

steps.title: Increasing yield with temperature means the forward reaction
steps.content: Increasing yield with temperature means the forward reaction is endothermic (ΔH +ve).

steps.title: Pressure not affecting yield means equal moles of
steps.content: Pressure not affecting yield means equal moles of gas on both sides: P+2Q (3 mol) ⇌ 2R+S (3 mol).

steps.title: The mixture s spot s align in Rf
steps.content: The mixture's spot(s) align in Rf value with dyes W and Y on the reference chromatogram (statement 3).

steps.content: Spot size/intensity doesn't reliably indicate exact relative concentration (statement 1 false), and the mobile phase is the water, not the paper (statement 2 false).

question: Two of the following equations represent redox reactions: 1: Cl2 + 2KI → I2 + 2KCl. 2: Cl2 + 2e⁻ → 2Cl⁻. 3: HCl + AgNO3 → AgCl + HNO3. 4: Cl2 + H2O → HCl + HClO. Which two equations represent redox reactions?

steps.content: Reaction 1: Cl is reduced (0→-1), one oxidised (-1→0) — redox.

steps.content: Reaction 4 is a disproportionation of Cl (0→-1 and 0→+1) — redox.

steps.content: Reaction 2 is only a half-equation; reaction 3 is a precipitation with no oxidation-state changes.

question: Copper reacts with concentrated nitric acid to produce copper(II) nitrate, water and compound X (contains no copper or hydrogen). The balanced equation shows 3 moles of copper producing 4 moles of water. What is the identity of compound X?

steps.title: Neutralisation volume test depends only on moles of
steps.content: Neutralisation volume (test 2) depends only on moles of acid, which is equal for both, so it doesn't distinguish strength.

Sodium sulfate was prepared by neutralising 25.0cm³ of 0.1 mol/dm³ NaOH with exactly 50.0cm³ of sulfuric acid. What is the concentration of the sulfuric acid in g/dm³? (Mr: H2SO4=98)

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.content: Moles NaOH $=0.0025$. $2NaOH+H_2SO_4\to Na_2SO_4+2H_2O$, so moles H2SO4 $=0.00125$ in 0.05dm^3: conc $=0.025$mol/dm^3 $=0.025\times98=2.45$g/dm^3.

question: During electrolysis of aqueous sodium sulfate: anode: $2H_2O\to O_2+4H^++4e^-$; cathode: $2H_2O+2e^-\to H_2+2OH^-$. Which deductions, if any, can be made? 1: the mole ratio of hydrogen to oxygen produced is 1:1. 2: the sodium sulfate solution becomes more concentrated as electrolysis proceeds. 3: the whole solution becomes acidic due to H⁺ at the anode.

steps.title: Per electrons mol O and mol H are
steps.content: Per 4 electrons: 1 mol O2 and 2 mol H2 are made, a 2:1 ratio (not 1:1, so 1 false).

steps.title: Water is consumed while dissolved Na SO remains
steps.content: Water is consumed while dissolved Na2SO4 remains, so the solution becomes more concentrated (2 true).

steps.title: H and OH are produced in equal amounts
steps.content: H⁺ and OH⁻ are produced in equal amounts and mix, keeping the bulk solution neutral (3 false).

question: Ethanol's structure is $CH_3CH_2OH$. Given $H_2C{=}CH_2(g)+H_2O(g)\to C_2H_5OH(g)$, $\Delta H=-45$kJ/mol, and mean bond energies (kJ/mol): H–H=+436, C–C=+346, C–H=+413, O–H=+464, C=C=+611, which option correctly gives the bond energy of the C–O bond in ethanol?

steps.title: Bonds broken C C C H bonds O
steps.content: Bonds broken: C=C + 4 C-H bonds + 2 O-H bonds $=611+1652+928=3191$.

steps.title: Bonds formed C C C H bonds O
steps.content: Bonds formed: C–C + 5 C-H bonds + O–H + C–O $=346+2065+464+x=2875+x$. $\Delta H=3191-(2875+x)=-45\Rightarrow x=361$kJ/mol.

question: Between 1954 and 1971 a mine in Northern Australia released excess copper into the nearby Finnis River, killing most fish. steps.content: Statement 2 is unsupported — the passage only states that other fish died, not that they lacked genetic variation.

steps.title: Statements and correctly describe natural selection actin
steps.content: Statements 1 and 3 correctly describe natural selection acting on existing variation.

question: A student used a light microscope at 40x magnification on a stained, non-dividing tissue sample. steps.content: The cell wall is a large, distinct structure visible even at low power (40x).

steps.content: Mitochondria are too small to resolve at this magnification with a standard light microscope, and chromosomes are not visible as the cell is non-dividing (chromatin uncondensed).

question: Given a DNA triplet code table, and the sequence …CGC AGT… An insertion occurs between the first and second bases; later a deletion removes one base from the sequence. Assuming this is part of a longer coding sequence and no other mutations occur, what are the probabilities that (i) after the first mutation, the first triplet does not code for arginine, and (ii) after the second mutation, both triplets code for arginine?

steps.title: An insertion of one of equally likely bases
steps.content: An insertion of one of 4 equally likely bases after the first base of CGC shifts the frame; checking against the code table, 3 of the 4 possible inserted bases give a first triplet that is not arginine (probability 3/4).

steps.title: The second probability follows from enumerating which single
steps.content: The second probability follows from enumerating which single-base deletions restore arginine-coding triplets at both positions.

question: A parachutist falls at terminal speed with the parachute open. Forces are labelled: weight of parachutist (R), force from parachute on parachutist (P), air resistance on parachutist (Q), force from parachutist on parachute (N), weight of parachute (M), air resistance on parachute (L). [DIAGRAM: free-body diagrams of the parachute and the parachutist showing forces L, M, N, P, Q, R] Consider equations: 1: L+M=N. 2: P+Q=R. 3: L=Q. 4: N=P. 5: L+Q=R+M. Which of these, if any, is/are a direct result of applying Newton's Third Law here?

steps.title: Newton s Third Law applies to the direct
steps.content: Newton's Third Law applies to the direct force pair between the parachute and the parachutist: the parachute pushes on the parachutist (P) and the parachutist pushes back on the parachute (N) — these are equal and opposite, so N=P.

steps.title: The other equations involve forces on different bodies
steps.content: The other equations involve forces on different bodies from a third party (air) or are equilibrium conditions, not N3L pairs directly.

steps.title: For P moles acid mol mol Mg so
steps.content: For P: moles acid $=1.0\times0.040=0.04$mol $<0.05$mol Mg, so acid is limiting, giving H$_2$=0.04mol.

steps.content: For Q (2.0 mol/dm$^3$, same volume): moles acid $=0.08$mol $>0.05$mol Mg, so Mg becomes limiting, H$_2$=0.05mol (higher volume than P, and faster due to higher concentration) — matches curve 3.

steps.title: For R mol dm moles acid mol mol
steps.content: For R (0.5 mol/dm$^3$): moles acid $=0.02$mol $<0.05$mol, acid limiting, H$_2$=0.02mol (lower volume than P, and slower) — matches curve 5.

question: Nitrogen and hydrogen react together to form ammonia as shown below: N₂(g) + 3H₂(g) → 2NH₃(g). The energy released by this reaction is 93 kJ mol⁻¹. What is the bond energy in the nitrogen molecule? (Bond energies: H–H = 436 kJ mol⁻¹; N–H = 391 kJ mol⁻¹)

steps.title: Bonds broken bonds formed
steps.content: $\Delta H=$ bonds broken $-$ bonds formed: $-93=(E_{N\equiv N}+3\times436)-(6\times391)=E_{N\equiv N}+1308-2346$.

steps.title: Statement is false binary fission produces genetically near
steps.content: Statement 1 is false: binary fission produces genetically near-identical daughter cells, not large genetic differences.

steps.title: Statement is false as worded bacteria prokaryotes divide
steps.content: Statement 2 is false as worded: bacteria (prokaryotes) divide by BINARY FISSION, not mitosis (mitosis is a eukaryotic process).

steps.content: Statement 3 is true: bacteria have no nucleus, so their circular chromosome replicates freely in the cytoplasm.

question:
Young plant roots show a similar positive response to the direction of gravity as their shoots do to the direction of light.
A clinostat can be used to counter the effects of gravity on a plant: it has a motor that slowly rotates a germinating seedling (mounted with its root against the wall of a horizontal drum, on moist cotton wool) about the drum's horizontal axis, and an opaque plastic cover prevents light from affecting the direction of root growth.
Two clinostats were set up using identical seedlings for two days: Clinostat 1 with the motor running, and Clinostat 2 with the motor stationary.
In which direction would the root grow in each clinostat?

steps.title: With the motor running clinostat the plant is
steps.content: With the motor running (clinostat 1), the plant is rotated so gravity's directional pull is averaged out in all directions, removing the gravitropic stimulus — the root continues growing in its original (horizontal) orientation along the tube.

steps.title: With the motor stationary clinostat normal positive gravi
steps.content: With the motor stationary (clinostat 2), normal positive gravitropism occurs and the root grows downwards.

steps.title: So clinostat grows horizontally and clinostat grows downw
steps.content: So clinostat 1 grows horizontally and clinostat 2 grows downwards.

question: A recessive-allele condition is shown in a family tree. [DIAGRAM: pedigree of affected/unaffected family members] One daughter has children with an unaffected man. For (i) father homozygous dominant and (ii) father heterozygous, what is the probability their first child is an affected girl?

steps.title: If the father is homozygous dominant no child
steps.content: If the father is homozygous dominant, no child can be homozygous recessive (probability 0).

steps.title: If heterozygous and the mother is a carrier
steps.content: If heterozygous and the mother is a carrier, $P(\text{affected})=\frac14$, $P(\text{girl})=\frac12$, giving $\frac18=0.125$.

question: Which one of the following is a simplification of $1-\left(\dfrac{3+\sqrt{3}}{6-2\sqrt{3}}\right)^{2}$?

steps.title: Factor the denominator and rationalise
steps.content: Write $\dfrac{3+\sqrt{3}}{6-2\sqrt{3}}=\dfrac{3+\sqrt{3}}{2(3-\sqrt{3})}$. Multiply numerator and denominator by $3+\sqrt{3}$: $\dfrac{(3+\sqrt{3})^{2}}{2(9-3)}=\dfrac{9+6\sqrt{3}+3}{12}=\dfrac{12+6\sqrt{3}}{12}=1+\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$.

question: The graph of $y=x^3+px^2+qx+6$ has a local maximum at $x=2$ and local minimum at $x=4$. Find p and q.

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: Has roots and so and
steps.content: $y'=3x^2+2px+q$ has roots 2 and 4: $3x^2+2px+q=3(x-2)(x-4)=3x^2-18x+24$, so $2p=-18\Rightarrow p=-9$, and $q=24$.

steps.title: Perpendicular lines have gradients multiplying to which e
steps.content: Perpendicular lines have gradients multiplying to $-1$: $(2p^2-p)(p-2)=-1$, which expands to $2p^3-5p^2+2p+1=0$.

steps.title: Testing gives so is a factor
The three real roots are the greatest is

question: A graph shows the horizontal force on a tennis ball (mass m) during a shot, as it reverses from speed u to speed v. [DIAGRAM: force vs time graph, force F between times t1 and t2] Which expression gives the magnitude of the ball's momentum as it leaves the racket?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: By the impulse momentum theorem taking the outgoing
steps.content: By the impulse-momentum theorem, taking the outgoing direction as positive: impulse $=F(t_2-t_1)=p_f-p_i=mv-(-mu)$, so $mv=F(t_2-t_1)-mu$.

question: A group of drivers, consisting of 200 women and 300 men, was asked if they passed their driving test at the first attempt. Altogether 167 of the group said they passed at the first attempt. Of the women, 143 said they did not pass at the first attempt. How many of the men said they passed at the first attempt?

steps.title: Men who passed total passed women who passed
steps.content: Men who passed = total passed - women who passed = 167 - 57 = 110.

subtopic: Cuboid volume vs surface area equation
question: A cuboid has sides of length $x$, $x\sqrt{2}$ and $2x$, measured in cm. The volume, in cm3, of the cuboid is numerically equal to twice the total surface area, in cm2, of the cuboid. What is the value of x?

steps.title: Write volume and surface area
steps.content: The sides are $x$, $x\sqrt{2}$ and $2x$. Volume: $$V = x \cdot x\sqrt{2} \cdot 2x = 2\sqrt{2}\, x^3.$$ Total surface area: $$S = 2\bigl(x\cdot x\sqrt{2} + x\cdot 2x + x\sqrt{2}\cdot 2x\bigr) = 2x^2\bigl(2 + 3\sqrt{2}\bigr).$$

question: The line joining the points with coordinates (p, p - 1) and (1 - p, 2p) is parallel to the line with equation 2x + 3y + 1 = 0. What is the value of p?

steps.title: Gradient of given line gradient
steps.content: Gradient of given line: $2x+3y+1=0 \Rightarrow y=-\tfrac{2}{3}x-\tfrac{1}{3}$, gradient $=-\tfrac{2}{3}$.

question: A rectangle PQRS is drawn inside a circle, with its vertices on the circumference of the circle. [DIAGRAM: rectangle PQRS inscribed in a circle]. The ratio of the length of PQ to the length of QR is 2:1. The area of the rectangle PQRS is 96 cm2. What is the radius, in cm, of the circle?

steps.title: The diagonal of the rectangle is a diameter
steps.content: The diagonal of the rectangle is a diameter: $\text{diag}^2 = (2a)^2+a^2 = 5a^2

question: The expected number of bottles of water sold in a day at a sports ground is directly proportional to the square of the average outside temperature, in degrees Celsius, for that day. On a day when the average outside temperature is 16°C, 64 bottles of water, the expected number, are sold. On a warmer day, when the average outside temperature is T°C, 256 bottles of water are sold, which is 31 bottles more than the expected number for that day. What is the value of T?

steps.title: Expected on the warmer day and actual sold
steps.content: Expected on the warmer day $=0.25T^2$, and actual sold $=256=$ expected $+31$, so expected $=225$.

question: At a cinema, drinks are sold in regular and large sizes. The cups for these are mathematically similar. The ratio of the heights of the cups and the ratio of the depths of the drinks are both 4:5. The volume of drink in a regular size cup is 320 cm3. The mean of n numbers is p. The mean of two of these numbers is q. The mean of the remaining numbers is 10. Which of the following is a correct expression for n in terms of p and q?

steps.title: Sum of the two numbers sum of remaining
steps.content: Sum of the two numbers $=2q$; sum of remaining $(n-2)$ numbers $=10(n-2)$.

question: The straight lines 5x + 2y = 20, y = 3x - 23, and x = 0 enclose a region with area K square units. What is the value of K?

steps.title: Find the three vertices gives gives gives
steps.content: Find the three vertices: $(5x+2y=20)\cap(y=3x-23)$ gives $(6,-5)$; $(5x+2y=20)\cap(x=0)$ gives $(0,10)$; $(y=3x-23)\cap(x=0)$ gives $(0,-23)$.

question: A scale model of a cylindrical pillar is to be made. The full-sized pillar has a volume of 12π m3. The model will use a length scale of 1:40. The model is to be a solid cylinder made of a plastic which has a density of 4/3 g cm-3. What is the mass of the model in grams?

steps.title: Volume scales as model volume
steps.content: Volume scales as $(\text{length scale})^3$: model volume $=12\pi \times \left(\tfrac{1}{40}\right)^3 \text{ m}^3 = \dfrac{12\pi \times 10^6}{64000}\text{ cm}^3 = 187.5\pi\text{ cm}^3$.

subtopic: Regular pentagon and equilateral triangle angles
question: PQRST is a regular pentagon [DIAGRAM: pentagon PQRST with equilateral triangle RSU constructed on side RS, external to the pentagon]. RSU is an equilateral triangle. What is the size of angle STU?

steps.title: Interior angle of a regular pentagon
steps.content: Interior angle of a regular pentagon $=108^\circ$.

steps.title: Triangle STU is isosceles with since relates via
steps.content: Triangle STU is isosceles with $ST = TU$ (since $TU$ relates via the pentagon side and equilateral triangle side, both equal to the pentagon's side length), and angle $TSU = 108^\circ - 60^\circ

steps.content: = 48^\circ$ (interior angle of pentagon at S minus the equilateral triangle's angle).

steps.title: Solving the isosceles triangle STU gives angle STU

Q is 5 km away from P on a bearing of 065°. R is 5 km away from Q on a bearing of 155°. What is the bearing of P from R?

steps.title: The turn from bearing to is exactly and
steps.content: The turn from bearing 065^\circ to 155^\circ is exactly 90^\circ, and PQ = QR = 5 km, so triangle PQR is an isosceles right angle triangle at Q.

steps.title: By symmetry the bearing of R from P
steps.content: By symmetry, the bearing of R from P is $065^\circ+45^\circ=110^\circ$, so the bearing of P from R is $110^\circ+180^\circ=290^\circ$.

The line segment RT is a tangent at the point S to a circle with centre O [DIAGRAM: circle with tangent RT at S, points Q and P on the circumference, angle PST = 75°]. Q and P are points on the circumference of the circle such that QS = QP. Angle PST = 75°. What is the size of angle QSO?

steps.title: By the tangent chord alternate segment angle theorem
steps.content: By the tangent-chord (alternate segment) angle theorem, the angle between tangent and chord equals the inscribed angle in the alternate segment.

steps.title: Combined with the isosceles condition QS QP and
steps.content: Combined with the isosceles condition QS = QP and OS perpendicular to the tangent RT (radius-tangent property), working through the angle chase gives angle QSO $= \tfrac12 \times 75^\circ = 37.5^\circ$.

question: The vertical height h cm of an isosceles triangle is 3 cm longer than the base length of b cm. The sloping side is of length s cm. The area of the triangle is 14 cm2. There is one value of s which satisfies these conditions. Within which range does this value of s lie?

steps.title: Area taking positive root so
steps.content: Area $=\tfrac12 b h = 14 \Rightarrow b(b+3)=28 \Rightarrow b^2+3b-28=0 \Rightarrow b=4$ (taking positive root), so $h=7$.
