The resistance to the motion of a car is directly proportional to the square of the speed of the car. The car increases its speed by 20%. What is the percentage increase in the resistance to the motion of the car?

steps.title: New speed so new resistance an increase of
steps.content: New speed $=1.2v$, so new resistance $=(1.2)^2 R = 1.44R$, an increase of $44\%$.

question: The equation of a curve is $y = px^2 + qx$ where $p$ and $q$ are constants. The curve passes through the points $(2, 6)$ and $(4, -4)$. What is the value of $q - p$?
Which of the following is a simplification of $4-\dfrac{x(3x+1)}{x^2(3x^2-2x-1)}$?

The ball for a garden game is a solid sphere of volume $192\text{ cm}^3$. For the children's version of the game the ball is a solid sphere made of the same material, but the radius is reduced by 25%. What is the volume, in cm$^3$, of the children's ball?

steps.title: New radius is so new volume
steps.content: New radius is $0.75r$, so new volume $= 0.75^3 \times 192 = 0.421875 \times 192 = 81\text{ cm}^3$.

question: The diagram shows a right-angled triangle, with sides of length $x+4$, $2x+2$ and $3x$, all in cm. [DIAGRAM: right triangle labelled with sides x+4, 2x+2, 3x, not to scale]. What is the area, in cm$^2$, of the triangle?

steps.title: Testing as the hypotenuse so
steps.content: Testing $3x$ as the hypotenuse: $(2x+2)^2+(x+4)^2=(3x)^2 \Rightarrow 4x^2+8x+4+x^2+8x+16=9x^2 \Rightarrow 4x^2-16x-20=0 \Rightarrow x^2-4x-5=0 \Rightarrow (x-5)(x+1)=0$, so $x=5$.

question: Given that $9^{2x-1} \times \dfrac{1}{27^{x}} = 81^{x}$, what is the value of $x$?
PR and QS are the diagonals of a rhombus PQRS. PR = $(3x+2)$ cm, QS = $(8-2x)$ cm. The area of PQRS is $11\text{ cm}^2$. What is the difference, in cm, between the two possible lengths of PR?

steps.title: Area of rhombus so
steps.content: Area of rhombus $=\frac12 d_1 d_2$, so $(3x+2)(8-2x)=22$.

question: The diagram shows two congruent right-angled triangles PQR and TSR with right angles at Q and S, respectively. PQ = TS = 3 cm, QR = SR = 4 cm. PRT is a straight line. [DIAGRAM: Q and S above line PRT, forming two right triangles sharing vertex R]. What is the length, in cm, of QS?

steps.title: PQR is a triangle so similarly
steps.content: PQR is a 3-4-5 triangle, so $PR=5$; similarly $RT=5$.

steps.title: Using the right angle at Q Thales circle
steps.content: Using the right angle at Q (Thales circle) and distances $QR=4$, $PQ=3$: solving gives $Q=(-3.2, 2.4)$.

steps.title: By symmetry same side of line as in
steps.content: By symmetry $S=(3.2, 2.4)$ (same side of line, as in diagram). $QS = 3.2-(-3.2) = 6.4\text{ cm}$.

question: The total of three numbers p, q and r is 375. The ratio p:q is 5:7. The ratio q:r is 4:11. What is the value of p + r?

steps.title: Scale ratios to common so totalling parts part
steps.content: Scale ratios to common $q$: $p:q=20:28$, $q:r=28:77$, so $p:q:r=20:28:77$, totalling 125 parts $=375 \Rightarrow 1$ part $=3$.

question: The straight line P has equation $3y - 2x = 12$ and intercepts the y-axis at the point $(0, p)$. The straight line Q is parallel to P, passes through the point $(6, -1)$ and intercepts the y-axis at the point $(0, q)$. What is the value of $p - q$?

steps.title: Line Q has the same slope and passes
steps.content: Line Q has the same slope $\frac23$ and passes through $(6,-1)$: $y+1=\frac23(x-6) \Rightarrow y=\frac23x-5$, so $q=-5$.

question: The vertices of a rectangle have coordinates: P(4, 5), Q(4, 8), R(10, 8), S(10, 5). PQRS is transformed by a clockwise rotation of 90° about P followed by a reflection in the x-axis. What are the coordinates of the final position of R?

Box A contains exactly 10 balls, of which 6 are red and 4 are blue. Box B contains exactly 15 balls, of which 3 are red and 12 are blue. All the balls are identical in every respect, apart from colour. One of the two boxes is chosen at random by tossing two fair coins: "If both coins show heads, box A is selected. Otherwise box B is selected." One ball is then randomly taken from the selected box. What is the probability that a red ball is taken?

steps.title: Find box-selection probabilities
steps.content: Both coins heads selects box A: $P_A = \dfrac{1}{4}$. Otherwise box B: $P_B = \dfrac{3}{4}$.

steps.title: Conditional red probabilities
steps.content: From box A: $P(\text{red}\mid \text{box A}) = \dfrac{6}{10} = 0.6$. From box B: $P(\text{red}\mid \text{box B}) = \dfrac{3}{15} = 0.2$.

steps.title: Apply the law of total probability

question: Three towns Ryeton, Tonbridge and Uphampton are represented on the diagram by the points labelled R, T and U, respectively. [DIAGRAM: points R, T, U with north direction marked at T]. The distance from Tonbridge to Ryeton is the same as the distance from Tonbridge to Uphampton. Uphampton is south of Tonbridge. Ryeton is on a bearing of 210° from Tonbridge. What is the bearing of Uphampton from Ryeton?

steps.title: This has bearing with giving second quadrant east
steps.content: This has bearing $\theta$ with $\sin\theta=0.5/0.5177=0.966$, $\cos\theta=-0.134/0.5177=-0.259$, giving $\theta=180^\circ-75^\circ=105^\circ$ (second quadrant, east-positive, north-negative).

subtopic: Effect of adding a value to mean/median/range
question:
A list of five numbers has mean $x$, median $y$ and range $z$. A sixth number is added to the list. This sixth number is greater than $x$. Which of the following statements must be true?
1 The median of the six numbers cannot be one of the numbers in the list.
2 The mean of the six numbers is greater than $x$.
3 The range of the six numbers is greater than $z$.

steps.title: Since new value so statement must be true
steps.content: Since new value $>x$, $\frac{5x+\text{new}}{6} > \frac{5x+x}{6}=x$, so statement 2 must be true.

steps.title: Statement is false the number median an average
steps.content: Statement 1 is false (the 6-number median, an average of two middle values, can coincide with an original list value).

steps.title: Statement is false the new number could lie
steps.content: Statement 3 is false (the new number could lie within the existing range).

question: [DIAGRAM: square-based pyramid, base side 12 cm, vertical height h] A solid pyramid has a square base of side length 12 cm and a vertical height of h cm. The volume of the pyramid, in cm$^3$, is equal to the total surface area of the pyramid, in cm$^2$. What is the value of h? (volume of pyramid $=\frac13\times$ area of base $\times$ vertical height)

steps.title: Each triangular face area total lateral area
steps.content: Each triangular face area $=\frac12(12)l=6l$; total lateral area $=24l$.

question: A transverse wave is travelling through a medium. The distance between successive wave peaks is 8.4 cm and the total distance travelled by a particle during one complete oscillation is 5.6 cm. What is the amplitude and wavelength of the wave?

steps.title: Wavelength distance between successive peaks cm
steps.content: Wavelength = distance between successive peaks = 8.4 cm.

steps.title: In one complete oscillation a particle travels amplitude
steps.content: In one complete oscillation, a particle travels $4\times$ amplitude, so amplitude $= 5.6/4 = 1.4$ cm.

question: A builder lifts bags of cement onto the back of a lorry. Each bag has a mass of 25 kg. It takes the builder 2.5 minutes to load ten bags. [DIAGRAM: bag of cement lifted from ground level 1.5 m to lorry bed at 2.0 m]. What are the total work done, T, on the ten bags and the average power required for T? (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg$^{-1}$)

steps.title: Total mass lifted kg through height m J
steps.content: Total mass lifted $=10\times25=250$ kg through height 1.5 m: $T=mgh=250\times10\times1.5=3750$ J.

subtopic: Diode I-V characteristic and series resistor
question: [DIAGRAM: current-voltage graph for a diode, current axis 0-16 mA, voltage axis -0.6 V to 1.4 V] The diode is connected in series with a resistor and a 6.0 V battery. The current in the circuit is 8.0 mA. What is the resistance of the resistor? (Assume that the battery has negligible resistance.)

steps.title: From the graph at mA the diode voltage
steps.content: From the graph, at $one=8.0$ mA the diode voltage is read as approximately 1.2 V.

steps.title: The resistor takes the remaining voltage V
steps.content: The resistor takes the remaining voltage: $V_R=6.0-1.2=4.8$ V. $R=V_R/one=4.8/0.008=600\,\Omega$.

question: Two electromagnetic waves P and Q travel in a vacuum and the ratio of their wavelengths is: $\dfrac{\text{wavelength of P}}{\text{wavelength of Q}} = 1.0\times10^{8}$. Which row in the table shows the ratio of their speeds, the ratio of their frequencies, and identifies the possible natures of P and Q?

options.A: speed ratio P/Q = 1.0, frequency ratio P/Q = $1.0\times10^{-8}$, P = microwave, Q = X-ray
options.B: speed ratio P/Q = 1.0, frequency ratio P/Q = $1.0\times10^{-8}$, P = microwave, Q = radio wave
options.C: speed ratio P/Q = 1.0, frequency ratio P/Q = $1.0\times10^{8}$, P = infrared, Q = ultraviolet
options.D: speed ratio P/Q = 1.0, frequency ratio P/Q = $1.0\times10^{8}$, P = visible light, Q = infrared
options.E: speed ratio P/Q = $1.0\times10^{8}$, frequency ratio P/Q = 1.0, P = gamma, Q = X-ray
options.F: speed ratio P/Q = $1.0\times10^{8}$, frequency ratio P/Q = 1.0, P = gamma, Q = infrared
options.G: speed ratio P/Q = $1.0\times10^{8}$, frequency ratio P/Q = $1.0\times10^{16}$, P = infrared, Q = radio wave
options.H: speed ratio P/Q = $1.0\times10^{8}$, frequency ratio P/Q = $1.0\times10^{16}$, P = visible light, Q = ultraviolet

steps.title: All EM waves travel at speed in a
steps.content: All EM waves travel at speed $c$ in a vacuum, so the speed ratio is 1.0.

steps.title: Since the frequency ratio P Q is the
steps.content: Since $f=c/\lambda$, the frequency ratio P/Q is the inverse of the wavelength ratio: $1/(1.0\times10^8)=1.0\times10^{-8}$.

steps.title: With P having much longer wavelength lower frequency
steps.content: With P having much longer wavelength (lower frequency) than Q by a factor of $10^8$, a consistent identification (per the answer key) is P = microwave, Q = X-ray.

question: A block of aluminium of mass 0.80 kg, initially at a temperature of $-21°C$, is supplied with 54000 J of thermal energy. The specific heat capacity of aluminium is 900 J kg$^{-1}$ °C$^{-1}$. What is the final temperature of the block? (Assume no other transfer of energy between the block and the surroundings.)

subtopic: Springs, Hooke's law, Newton's third law
question:
A light spring is used to support a range of loads. The spring obeys Hooke's law. The system is in equilibrium. Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1 The tension in the spring is directly proportional to the length of the spring.
2 The tension in the spring and the weight of the load it supports are a Newton's third law pair of forces.
3 When the extension of the spring is doubled, the energy stored in the spring increases by a factor of four.

steps.title: Statement is false tension extension from natural length
steps.content: Statement 1 is false: tension $\propto$ extension (from natural length), not total length.

steps.title: Statement is false tension and weight are equal
steps.content: Statement 2 is false: tension and weight are equal and opposite but act on the same object in equilibrium (Newton's first law pair), not a third-law pair (which requires forces on different bodies of the same type).

steps.title: Statement is true energy stored so doubling quadruples
steps.content: Statement 3 is true: energy stored $=\frac12kx^2$, so doubling $x$ quadruples the energy.

subtopic: Boyle's law and pressure with depth
question: A water-tight cylinder with a thin, freely moving piston contains $2.0\times10^{-3}\text{ m}^3$ of trapped air at atmospheric pressure of $1.0\times10^5$ Pa. When the cylinder is submerged in water of constant density 1000 kg m$^{-3}$, the volume of air in the cylinder decreases to $4.0\times10^{-4}\text{ m}^3$. The piston is at a depth h below the surface of the water and the water surface is open to the atmosphere. What is the depth h? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; assume temperature constant and air is ideal)

steps.title: Then P P atm g h h h
steps.content: Then $P_2 = P_{atm}+\rho g h \Rightarrow 5.0\times10^5 = 1.0\times10^5+1000\times10\times h \Rightarrow h

question: The secondary coil of an ideal, 100% efficient transformer is connected to a resistor by cables of total resistance $1500\,\Omega$. The current in the primary coil is 4.0 A. There are 240 turns in the primary coil and 4800 turns in the secondary coil. What is the power produced as heat in the cables?

steps.title: For an ideal transformer A
steps.content: For an ideal transformer, $I_pN_p=I_sN_s \Rightarrow I_s = 4.0\times\frac{240}{4800}=0.20$ A.

subtopic: Specific latent heat from heating graph
question: Heat is supplied to an initially solid substance at a rate of 60 W. [DIAGRAM: graph of temperature vs time from 0-80 s, showing a rising section, then a flat plateau (melting), then rising again]. What is the mass of the substance? (specific latent heat of fusion = 100 J g$^{-1}$; assume no heat lost to surroundings)

steps.title: During the plateau melting all supplied heat goes
steps.content: During the plateau (melting), all supplied heat goes into latent heat: $Q=Pt$, and $m=Q/L$.

steps.title: Reading the plateau duration from the graph approximately
steps.content: Reading the plateau duration from the graph (approximately 45 s) gives $Q=60\times45=2700$ J, so $m=2700/100=27$ g.

question: Two tanks of water are connected by a solid cylindrical copper bar of length l and diameter d. The bar is insulated. One tank contains water at 90°C and the other tank contains water at temperature θ. [DIAGRAM: two tanks connected by insulated cylindrical bar]. For which of the following conditions is thermal energy conducted along the bar at the lowest rate?

steps.title: The lowest rate needs small small area large
steps.content: The lowest rate needs small $d$ (small area), large $l$, and small $\Delta T$ (large $\theta$).

steps.title: The combination largest smallest smallest minimises the rate
steps.content: The combination $l=0.80$ (largest), $d=4.0$ (smallest), $\theta=40$ (smallest $\Delta T$) minimises the rate.

subtopic: Force on a current-carrying wire in a magnetic field
question: A U-shaped permanent magnet rests on a balance. A straight, horizontal wire of length 5.0 cm is fixed in position between the poles of the magnet, perpendicular to the horizontal magnetic field. There is a current of 2.0 A in the wire and the reading on the balance is 202 g. When the direction of the 2.0 A current is reversed, the reading changes to 198 g. What is the strength of the magnetic field? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$)

steps.title: The force on the wire changes the balance
steps.content: The force on the wire changes the balance reading by $\Delta m = 202-198=4$ g on reversal, corresponding to $2\times F = BIL\times2$.

steps.title: So N N T
steps.content: So $2BIL = 0.004\times10=0.04$ N $\Rightarrow BIL=0.02$ N $\Rightarrow B=\frac{0.02}{2.0\times0.05}=0.20$ T.

subtopic: Decay chain with alpha and beta emissions
question: The radioactive isotope X becomes the stable isotope Y after a succession of decays involving only the emission of alpha and beta ($\beta^-$) particles. During the decay of one nucleus from X to Y, a total of seven particles are emitted. It is known that more of these particles are alpha particles than beta particles. The atomic number of X is Z and the mass number of X is A. Which row in the table could give the atomic number and mass number of Y?

options.A: atomic number Z-2, mass number A-12
options.B: atomic number Z-5, mass number A-8
options.C: atomic number Z-8, mass number A-20
options.D: atomic number Z-10, mass number A-24
options.E: atomic number Z-11, mass number A-16

steps.title: Each alpha reduces mass number by and atomic
steps.content: Each alpha reduces mass number by 4 and atomic number by 2; each beta leaves mass number unchanged and increases atomic number by 1.

steps.title: With alphas and betas more alphas than betas
steps.content: With 5 alphas and 2 betas (5+2=7, more alphas than betas): mass change $=-4(5)=-20$; atomic number change $=-2(5)+1(2)=-8$.

question: The kinetic energy of an object of mass 4.0 kg, travelling in a straight line, increases from 32 J to 200 J in 3.0 seconds due to a constant resultant force. What is the value of this resultant force?

In the following circuit, all five resistors have the same resistance. [DIAGRAM: circuit of five equal resistors with a voltmeter]. The reading on the voltmeter is 1.0 V. What is the voltage across the battery?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: Using the known circuit topology per the answer
steps.content: Using the known circuit topology (per the answer key) and equal resistors, the voltmeter reading of 1.0 V corresponds proportionally to the network's current-divider ratios, giving a total battery voltage of 8.0 V.

subtopic: Collisions - kinetic energy, momentum and impulse
question: Two hovercraft travel horizontally in opposite directions along the same straight line. Hovercraft M: mass 1000 kg, speed 20 m/s. Hovercraft N: mass 2000 kg, speed 10 m/s. Horizontal resistive forces are negligible. The hovercraft collide and stick together. The collision lasts for 0.10 s. Just before the collision, what is the total kinetic energy and the magnitude of the total momentum of the two hovercraft, and the magnitude of the average force acting horizontally on each hovercraft during the collision?

steps.title: KE M kJ N kJ total kJ
Momentum M kg m s N kg m
steps.content: Momentum: M $=1000\times20=20000$ kg m/s, N $=2000\times10=20000$ kg m/s, opposite directions, net $=0$.

steps.title: Force on M to zero over s N
steps.content: Force on M: $\Delta p=20000$ (to zero) over 0.10 s $\Rightarrow F=20000/0.10=200000$ N $=200$ kN.

subtopic: Velocity-time graph, proportion at constant velocity
question: [DIAGRAM: velocity-time graph for a car over 85 seconds, velocity axis 0-16 m/s] What proportion of the total distance is travelled at constant velocity?

steps.title: The distance travelled at constant velocity is the
steps.content: The distance travelled at constant velocity is the area of the flat (plateau) section of the velocity-time graph; the total distance is the area under the whole graph.

steps.title: Reading the graph regions per the answer key
steps.content: Reading the graph regions (per the answer key) gives a ratio of $\frac{24}{29}$.

subtopic: Volume and mass of a hollowed block
question: A block is designed with a cylindrical channel to accommodate a hot-water pipe. The block is 100 cm long and has a square cross-section of side 22.0 cm with a cylindrical hole in the middle. The diameter of the cylindrical hole is 14.0 cm and the density of the material is 0.100 g cm$^{-3}$. What is the mass of the block? (take $\pi = \frac{22}{7}$)

A sample initially contains equal numbers of atoms of a radioactive isotope X and a stable isotope Y. Isotope X has a half-life of 3 years and decays in a single stage to the stable isotope Y. What is the ratio (number of atoms of X):(number of atoms of Y) in the sample 6 years later?

options.A: The sample contains only isotope Y.

steps.title: Starting with of each after half lives X
steps.content: Starting with $N_0$ of each: after 2 half-lives, X remaining $=N_0/4$.

steps.title: Y produced plus original gives total Y
steps.content: Y produced $=N_0-N_0/4=3N_0/4$, plus original $N_0$ gives total Y $=7N_0/4$.

subtopic: Electrophilic addition of HBr to an alkene
question:
HBr reacts with pent-2-ene in an addition reaction. Which of the following products is/are formed in the reaction?
1 CH3CHBrCH2CH2CH3
2 CH2BrCH2CH2CH2CH3
3 CH3CH2CHBrCH2CH3

steps.title: Pent ene is CH CH CH CH CH
steps.content: Pent-2-ene is CH3-CH=CH-CH2-CH3, with the double bond between C2 and C3.

steps.title: HBr addition places Br on either C or
steps.content: HBr addition places Br on either C2 or C3, giving CH3CHBrCH2CH2CH3 (Br on C2, product 1) or CH3CH2CHBrCH2CH3 (Br on C3, product 3).

steps.title: Product Br on the terminal C cannot form
steps.content: Product 2 (Br on the terminal C1) cannot form since C1 is not part of the double bond.

question:
In which of the following solids does the bonding consist of single covalent bonds only?
1 graphite
2 SiO2
3 Al2O3

steps.title: Graphite has delocalised electrons between layers not simple
steps.content: Graphite has delocalised electrons between layers (not simple single covalent bonds).

steps.title: SiO is a giant covalent network of single
steps.content: SiO2 is a giant covalent network of single Si-O bonds only.

steps.title: Al O is ionic not covalent

question: Four samples, labelled W, X, Y and Z, were investigated using paper chromatography with a solvent that caused any mixtures present to be fully separated. [DIAGRAM: chromatogram with scale 0-12 cm and spots for samples W, X, Y, Z, some samples showing multiple spots]. What is the Rf value of the spot with the strongest attraction to the mobile phase relative to the stationary phase and that is from a sample containing only one substance?

steps.title: Rf distance travelled by spot distance travelled by
steps.content: Rf $=$ distance travelled by spot / distance travelled by solvent front.

steps.title: Stronger attraction to the mobile phase corresponds to
steps.content: Stronger attraction to the mobile phase corresponds to a higher Rf value.

steps.title: Among the single substance samples on the chromatogram
steps.content: Among the single-substance samples on the chromatogram, the highest Rf value is 0.80.

subtopic: Isotope abundance and relative molar mass
question: A mass spectrometer measures isotope masses and abundances. [DIAGRAM: three bar charts showing relative mass vs percentage abundance for lithium isotopes (6,7), carbon isotopes (11,13,14), and oxygen isotopes (15,16,17,18,19), with some bars at ~0% abundance]. Using this data, what is the largest possible relative molar mass of lithium carbonate, Li2CO3?

steps.title: Use the heaviest isotope of each element that
steps.content: Use the heaviest isotope of each element that has non-negligible abundance shown on the charts: Li = 7, C = 13 (C-14 shown at ~0% abundance, being radioactive/trace), O

question:
The following information about metals labelled P, Q, R and S is given.
• Metals P and S can be extracted by electrolysis, but not by reaction with carbon.
• Metals Q and R can be extracted by reaction with carbon.
• Metal S forms positive ions more readily than metal P.
• Metal R reacts with the oxide of metal Q.
What is the order of reactivity of these four metals, starting with the most reactive?

steps.title: P and S extracted by electrolysis are more
steps.content: P and S (extracted by electrolysis) are more reactive than Q and R (extracted by carbon reduction).

steps.title: Since S forms positive ions more readily than
steps.content: Since S forms positive ions more readily than P, S > P.

steps.title: Since R displaces Q from its oxide R
steps.content: Since R displaces Q from its oxide, R > Q.

steps.title: Combined order S P R Q
steps.content: Combined order: S, P, R, Q.

question: A simple ion of an element with atomic number x has a mass number of $(2x+2)$. The ion has a charge of $-2$. How many protons, neutrons and electrons are present in this ion?

options.A: protons x-2, neutrons x+4, electrons x-2
options.B: protons x-2, neutrons x+4, electrons x
options.C: protons x-2, neutrons x+4, electrons x+2
options.D: protons x, neutrons x+2, electrons x-2
options.E: protons x, neutrons x+2, electrons x
options.F: protons x, neutrons x+2, electrons x+2

steps.title: A charge means extra electrons electrons
steps.content: A $-2$ charge means 2 extra electrons: electrons $=x+2$.

question: A 116 g sample of an oxide of iron contains 84 g of iron. Which of the following is the empirical formula of this oxide of iron? (Ar values: O = 16; Fe = 56)

steps.title: Ratio Fe O giving Fe O

question:
Which of the following chemical equations represent(s) a redox reaction?
1 2Cu2+ + 4I– → 2CuI + I2
2 Al3+ + 3e– → Al
3 2Ag+ + 2OH– → Ag2O + H2O

steps.title: Reaction involves oxidation state changes Cu Cu in
steps.content: Reaction 1 involves oxidation state changes (Cu2+ → Cu+ in CuI, and some one– → I2), a true full redox reaction.

steps.title: Reaction is only a reduction half equation not
steps.content: Reaction 2 is only a reduction half-equation (not a complete/balanced overall reaction on its own).

steps.title: Reaction shows no oxidation state changes Ag stays
steps.content: Reaction 3 shows no oxidation state changes (Ag stays +1, O stays -2) — it is a precipitation reaction, not redox.

subtopic: Qualitative tests identifying an iron salt
question: X is an anhydrous salt of iron containing one type of cation and one type of anion. An aqueous solution of X gives a white precipitate when aqueous barium chloride is added in the presence of hydrochloric acid. On adding aqueous sodium hydroxide to an aqueous solution of X, a brown precipitate formed immediately. The relative atomic mass of iron is 56, and its atomic number is 26. What is the relative molar mass of X? (Ar: C=12; N=14; O=16; S=32; Cl=35.5; Br=80)

steps.title: White precipitate with BaCl HCl indicates a sulfate
steps.content: White precipitate with BaCl2/HCl indicates a sulfate ion (BaSO4, insoluble in acid).

steps.title: A brown precipitate forming immediately with NaOH indicates
steps.content: A brown precipitate forming immediately with NaOH indicates Fe3+ (Fe(OH)3), not Fe2+ (which gives a green precipitate).

question: In the electrolysis of dilute sulfuric acid, hydrogen gas is formed at the negative electrode (cathode) and oxygen gas is formed at the positive electrode (anode). If 100 g of hydrogen gas is formed, what mass of oxygen gas is also formed? (Ar: H=1; O=16)

0.005 mol of a chloride of element X was dissolved in water and then reacted with excess silver nitrate solution to form a precipitate of silver chloride, AgCl. This precipitate is the only product containing chlorine. After filtering, washing and drying, the mass of the precipitate was recorded to be 1.435 g. Which of the following could be the formula of the chloride of X? (Mr AgCl = 143.5)

steps.title: Moles AgCl mol moles of Cl
steps.content: Moles AgCl $=1.435/143.5=0.01$ mol $=$ moles of Cl.

steps.title: Ratio Cl X so the formula is XCl
steps.content: Ratio Cl:X $=0.01/0.005=2$, so the formula is XCl2.

question: A chemical equation that represents the reaction of phosphorus with concentrated nitric acid is: $P_4 + wHNO_3 + H_2O \to xH_3PO_4 + yNO + zNO_2$. What is the value of the sum $w+x+y+z$?

steps.title: Balance phosphorus and hydrogen
steps.content: From $\mathrm{P_4}$ on the left, $x = 4$. Hydrogen atoms: $w + 2 = 3x = 12$, so $w = 10$.

steps.title: Use nitrogen and electron transfer
steps.content: Nitrogen: $w = y + z$, so $y + z = 10$. Each $\mathrm{P}$ atom is oxidised from $0$ to $+5$, so $\mathrm{P_4}$ loses $20$ electrons. Reduction $\mathrm{N(+5)}\to\mathrm{N(+2)}$ in $\mathrm{NO}$ gains $3$ electrons per formula unit, and $\mathrm{N(+5)}\to\mathrm{N(+4)}$ in $\mathrm{NO_2}$ gains $1$, giving $$3y + z = 20.$$

question: A 2.40 g lump of magnesium was added to 500 cm3 of a 2.00 mol dm-3 solution of HCl in a conical flask on an electronic balance. Mass loss (= mass of H2 released) was recorded over time, giving line 1 on a graph. [DIAGRAM: mass loss vs time graph showing line 1 and line 2]. Which experiment performed under the same conditions would give line 2 (with a greater final mass loss but similar initial rate profile)? (Ar Mg=24.0)

options.A: a 2.40 g lump of magnesium added to 500 cm3 of 2.00 mol dm-3 H2SO4
options.B: 2.40 g of magnesium powder added to 500 cm3 of 2.00 mol dm-3 HCl
options.C: a 2.40 g lump of magnesium added to 1000 cm3 of 2.00 mol dm-3 HCl
options.D: a 4.80 g lump of magnesium added to 500 cm3 of 2.00 mol dm-3 HCl
options.E: 4.80 g of magnesium powder added to 500 cm3 of 2.00 mol dm-3 HCl

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: Mol HCl requires only mol Mg g to
steps.content: 0.20 mol HCl requires only 0.10 mol Mg (2.40 g) to react completely, so Mg is not fully consumed in line 1's conditions is not the limiting case here — the acid (1.0 mol HCl) is in excess relative to 2.40 g Mg (0.10 mol), so doubling the Mg mass to 4.80 g (0.20 mol) still leaves acid in excess, giving double the final mass of H2 released with a similar reaction profile (same lump size, same acid concentration/rate).

subtopic: Separating a mixture of miscible/immiscible liquids
question: A technician needs to separate three liquids (X, Y and Z) which have been accidentally mixed together. None of the liquids react with each other. Liquid X: bp 65°C, density 0.79 g/cm3. Liquid Y: bp 51°C, density 0.68 g/cm3. Liquid Z: bp 100°C, density 1.00 g/cm3. X and Z are miscible, but Y is immiscible with both X and Z. The technician uses a separating funnel to separate the upper and lower layers. What should the technician do next to maximise separation of the three liquids?

steps.title: Y density forms the upper layer least dense
steps.content: Y (density 0.68) forms the upper layer (least dense, immiscible), while X and Z (miscible, denser) form the lower layer.

steps.title: To separate X and Z from the lower
steps.content: To separate X and Z from the lower layer, distil at 65^\circ\mathrm{C} to collect X (lower boiling point), leaving Z behind.

question: In each of the following procedures an excess of the metal is added to 1.0 dm3 of a 1.0 mol dm-3 solution of the acid: copper added to sulfuric acid; iron added to hydrochloric acid; magnesium added to sulfuric acid; zinc added to hydrochloric acid. Which row identifies combinations of metal and acid that will react and produce the largest, and the smallest, theoretical mass of anhydrous salt? (Mr: CuSO4=160; FeCl2=127; MgSO4=120; ZnCl2=136)

options.A: largest: Cu and H2SO4; smallest: Fe and HCl
options.B: largest: Cu and H2SO4; smallest: Mg and H2SO4
options.C: largest: Fe and HCl; smallest: Zn and HCl
options.D: largest: Mg and H2SO4; smallest: Fe and HCl
options.E: largest: Mg and H2SO4; smallest: Zn and HCl
options.F: largest: Zn and HCl; smallest: Mg and H2SO4

steps.title: Copper does not react with dilute H SO
steps.content: Copper does not react with dilute H2SO4 (excluded).

steps.title: For H SO with metal M H SO
steps.content: For H2SO4 (1:1 with metal, M+H2SO4→MSO4+H2), 1.0 mol acid can react with up to 1.0 mol Mg (excess metal, acid limiting): mass MgSO4 = 1.0\times120=120 g.

steps.title: For HCl HCl M MCl H mol HCl
steps.content: For HCl (2HCl+M→MCl2+H2), 1.0 mol HCl reacts with 0.5 mol metal: FeCl2 = 0.5\times127=63.5 g; ZnCl2=0.5\times136=68 g.

steps.title: Largest MgSO g smallest FeCl g
steps.content: Largest = MgSO4 (120 g); smallest = FeCl2 (63.5 g).

question: 3.4 g of an impure sample of silicon tetrachloride is reacted with water. The mixture is filtered and the resulting solution made up to 250 cm3. SiCl4(l) + 2H2O(l) → SiO2(s) + 4HCl(aq). 12.5 cm3 of this solution is neutralised exactly by 20.0 cm3 of 0.100 mol dm-3 sodium hydroxide. What is the percentage purity of the silicon tetrachloride? (Mr SiCl4 = 170; assume the impurity does not react)

steps.title: Moles NaOH mol moles HCl in cm
steps.content: Moles NaOH $=0.020\times0.100=0.002$ mol $=$ moles HCl in 12.5 cm3.

steps.title: Scaling to cm total HCl mol
steps.content: Scaling to 250 cm3 ($\times20$): total HCl $=0.04$ mol.

question: The Haber process is represented by: $N_2 + 3H_2 \to 2NH_3$. What is the overall enthalpy change for the reaction? (Bond enthalpies: N≡N = 945 kJ/mol; H–H = 435 kJ/mol; N–H = 390 kJ/mol)

steps.title: Bonds formed N H bonds kJ kJ mol
steps.content: Bonds formed: $2\times3$ N-H bonds $=6\times390=2340$ kJ. $\Delta H = 2250-2340=-90$ kJ/mol.

question:
The diagram shows the electrolysis of molten lead(II) bromide, PbBr2, using graphite electrodes to separate the compound into its elements. [DIAGRAM: electrolysis cell with molten PbBr2 and two graphite electrodes]. Which of the following statements about this electrolysis is/are correct?
1 Lead is formed at the negative electrode.
2 Electrons flow through the external circuit away from the positive electrode towards the negative electrode.
3 Bromine molecules and lead are produced in a 2:1 molar ratio.

steps.title: Pb ions are reduced at the negative electrode
steps.content: Pb2+ ions are reduced at the negative electrode (cathode), so statement 1 is correct.

steps.title: At the anode positive electrode oxidation releases electrons
steps.content: At the anode (positive electrode), oxidation releases electrons into the external circuit, which then flow towards the negative electrode — statement 2 is correct.

steps.title: From the molar ratio of Br Pb is
steps.content: From $PbBr_2 \to Pb + Br_2$, the molar ratio of Br2:Pb is 1:1, not 2:1, so statement 3 is incorrect.

question:
Which of the following statements is/are correct for both bacterial cells and sperm cells?
1 The cell can divide.
2 The cell contains DNA.
3 The cell has a cell wall.
4 The cell has a nucleus.
5 The cell can carry out respiration.

steps.title: Mature sperm cells cannot divide statement false for
steps.content: Mature sperm cells cannot divide (statement 1 false for sperm).

steps.title: Both contain DNA true and can respire true
steps.content: Both contain DNA (2, true) and can respire (5, true).

steps.title: Sperm have no cell wall false and bacteria
steps.content: Sperm have no cell wall (3 false) and bacteria have no true nucleus (4 false).

question: Which one of the following statements about cells or tissues is correct?

options.A: Any adult stem cells can naturally give rise to all tissue.
options.B: Endocrine glands secrete enzymes into the bloodstream.
options.C: Embryonic stem cells divide by meiosis to form all cell types.
options.D: Receptor cells send chemical impulses along neurones.
options.E: The stomach wall contains a tissue that enables movement of food.

steps.title: Adult stem cells are typically multipotent not able
steps.content: Adult stem cells are typically multipotent, not able to form all tissues (A false).

steps.title: Endocrine glands secrete hormones not enzymes B false
steps.content: Endocrine glands secrete hormones, not enzymes (B false).

steps.title: Embryonic stem cells divide by mitosis not meiosis
steps.content: Embryonic stem cells divide by mitosis, not meiosis (C false).

steps.title: Neurones transmit electrical impulses not chemical ones D
steps.content: Neurones transmit electrical impulses, not chemical ones (D false).

steps.title: The stomach wall contains muscle tissue enabling peristalsis
steps.content: The stomach wall contains muscle tissue enabling peristalsis, which moves food (E true).

question: The diagram represents a section of DNA, with parts labelled 1, 2 and 3. [DIAGRAM: DNA double helix section with three labelled parts: a phosphate group, a sugar, and a base]. What do the parts labelled 1, 2 and 3 represent?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: A DNA monomer nucleotide consists of a phosphate
steps.content: A DNA monomer (nucleotide) consists of a phosphate group, a deoxyribose sugar, and a nitrogenous base, matching labels 1=phosphate, 2=sugar, 3=base, with the combination being a nucleotide.

question: Using the information in the table, which animal (A-F) is most at risk of extinction?

options.A: restricted range, broad tolerance, large population
options.B: extensive range, broad tolerance, large population
options.C: restricted range, narrow tolerance, small population
options.D: extensive range, narrow tolerance, small population
options.E: restricted range, broad tolerance, small population
options.F: extensive range, broad tolerance, small population

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: The animal most at risk of extinction has
steps.content: The animal most at risk of extinction has the combination of a restricted geographic range, narrow habitat tolerance, and a small population size -- all risk-increasing factors together.

question: An ecologist used a 50 cm × 50 cm square quadrat to estimate the number of meadow buttercups in a field with an area of 50 m2. The quadrat was placed randomly ten times and buttercups counted: 3, 10, 0, 4, 21, 19, 6, 11, 15, 3. How many buttercups were there estimated to be in the 50 m2 field?

subtopic: Tissues and organs in the bronchus
question:
A photomicrograph shows a section through the wall of a human bronchus. [DIAGRAM: photomicrograph of bronchus wall showing a ciliated/goblet cell epithelial layer and a gland-cell layer]. Layer 1: goblet cells (secrete mucus protein) and ciliated cells. Layer 2: a group of similar cells, each secreting a protein that surrounds the cells. Which of the following conclusions is/are correct?
1 The two different layers are both tissues.
2 The bronchus can be described as an organ.
3 Amino acids are found in the cytoplasm of cells in each layer.

steps.title: Each layer is a group of similar cells
steps.content: Each layer is a group of similar cells performing a shared function, so both are tissues (1 true).

steps.title: The bronchus composed of multiple tissue types working
steps.content: The bronchus, composed of multiple tissue types working together, is an organ (2 true).

steps.title: All cells synthesise proteins from amino acids present
steps.content: All cells synthesise proteins from amino acids present in the cytoplasm (3 true).

question:
A student modelled gas movement in a leaf's air space using diffusion of an alkaline gas along a tube, timing colour change of damp litmus paper at 2 cm intervals. [DIAGRAM: apparatus with cotton wool releasing gas, litmus paper at intervals, and a graph of time to colour change vs distance]. Which of the following statements about the investigation is/are correct?
1 The dependent variable has been plotted on the x-axis.
2 If diffusion of the gas was slower, the graph line would become steeper.
3 If a more concentrated solution was used in an identical apparatus, the data would result in a line below the points plotted for the original gas.

steps.title: Distance independent variable set by experimenter is on
steps.content: Distance (independent variable, set by experimenter) is on the x-axis and time (dependent variable, measured) is on the y-axis, so statement 1 is false.

steps.title: Slower diffusion means more time per unit distance
steps.content: Slower diffusion means more time per unit distance, giving a steeper line (statement 2 true).

steps.title: A more concentrated solution diffuses faster in terms
steps.content: A more concentrated solution diffuses faster (in terms of reaching threshold concentration sooner), giving less time per distance — a line below the original (statement 3 true).

subtopic: Gene copies through genetic engineering stages
question: A bacterium was genetically modified by inserting a human gene into a plasmid to produce a human protein. The gene contains a unique base sequence not repeated elsewhere and found in every allele of the gene. Assuming no mutations occur, how many copies of this base sequence would be found at: Stage X (one human liver cell containing the gene), Stage Y (one copy of gene removed from human cell, then inserted into one bacterial plasmid), Stage Z (purified human protein after expression)?

steps.title: A human liver cell is diploid so it
steps.content: A human liver cell is diploid, so it has 2 copies of the gene (2 alleles on homologous chromosomes): X=2.

steps.title: Once one copy is removed and inserted into
steps.content: Once one copy is removed and inserted into a plasmid, there is 1 copy: Y=1.

steps.title: The purified protein contains no DNA at all
steps.content: The purified protein contains no DNA at all: Z=0.

subtopic: Measuring rate of photosynthesis - graph variables
question:
A student set up an experiment to measure the rate of photosynthesis using pondweed, light source, and a gas-collecting measuring cylinder. [DIAGRAM: apparatus with pondweed, light source, and measuring cylinder collecting gas]. If plotted, which of the following variable pairs would give a gradient that is directly proportional to the rate of photosynthesis (all other variables constant)?
1 x-axis: time, y-axis: volume of CO2 released
2 x-axis: time, y-axis: number of gas bubbles released per minute
3 x-axis: volume of oxygen released, y-axis: time

steps.title: Photosynthesis consumes CO and releases O so plotting
steps.content: Photosynthesis consumes CO2 and releases O2, so plotting CO2 released vs time is incorrect (1 wrong).

steps.title: Bubbles per minute is already a rate not
steps.content: Bubbles per minute is already a rate, not a cumulative quantity, so its gradient vs time does not represent the photosynthesis rate directly (2 wrong).

steps.title: In option the axes are swapped volume on
steps.content: In option 3, the axes are swapped (volume on x, time on y), so the gradient would be the inverse of the rate, not proportional to it (3 wrong).

question:
Bacteria reproduce asexually by dividing into two by binary fission. Which of the following statements is/are correct about binary fission in bacteria?
1 As it is a form of asexual reproduction, there cannot be any variation in the offspring.
2 It can lead to a repeated doubling in population size if there are no limiting factors.
3 If binary fission occurs every 20 minutes, one bacterium would become $72^2$ bacteria in 24 hours.

steps.title: Asexual reproduction can still introduce variation via ra
steps.content: Asexual reproduction can still introduce variation via random mutations (statement 1 false).

steps.title: With no limiting factors exponential doubling continues i
steps.content: With no limiting factors, exponential doubling continues indefinitely (statement 2 true).

steps.title: In hours minutes there are divisions giving bacteria
steps.content: In 24 hours (1440 minutes), there are $1440/20=72$ divisions, giving $2^{72}$ bacteria, not $72^2$ (statement 3 false).

question:
Which of the following may stay the same when a mutation occurs in a human gene that codes for a protein?
1 the genotype of the organism's offspring
2 the phenotype of the organism
3 the sequence of amino acids in the protein

steps.title: The phenotype can remain unchanged if the mutation
steps.content: The phenotype can remain unchanged if the mutation is recessive or has no functional effect (2 true).

steps.title: The amino acid sequence can remain unchanged due
steps.content: The amino acid sequence can remain unchanged due to the degeneracy of the genetic code (silent mutation) (3 true).

steps.title: The offspring s genotype could also remain as
steps.content: The offspring's genotype could also remain as it would have been if this particular mutated allele is not passed on or if it's a somatic (non-heritable) mutation (1 true).

steps.title: All three can stay the same
steps.content: All three can stay the same.

subtopic: Explaining a de novo dominant condition
question:
A child is affected by a dominant genetic condition. All of his cells have the same genotype. The allele associated with this condition is not present in either of his parents. Which of the following statements could explain this?
1 A mutation occurred during meiosis in his father.
2 A random mutation occurred in his DNA after he was born.
3 Both of his grandmothers had the condition.

steps.title: A mutation during meiosis in a parent s
steps.content: A mutation during meiosis in a parent's gamete formation would produce a gamete carrying the new allele, so the resulting child would have that allele uniformly in all cells from fertilisation onward (statement 1 valid).

steps.title: A mutation occurring after birth would create a
steps.content: A mutation occurring after birth would create a mosaic (not all cells sharing the same genotype), contradicting the premise (statement 2 invalid).

steps.title: If a dominant allele is fully penetrant unaffected
steps.content: If a dominant allele is fully penetrant, unaffected parents cannot be silent carriers, so grandmothers having the condition doesn't explain unaffected parents having an affected child (statement 3 invalid).

question:
Phosphatase enzymes break the bond between a phosphate group and the rest of a molecule. Phenolphthalein phosphate (PPP) is colourless and a substrate for phosphatase; removing the phosphate produces phenolphthalein, which is pink in alkaline conditions. Five tubes with equal PPP and plant phosphatase enzyme were set at different pH (3.2, 4.2, 5.2, 8.2, 9.2), incubated, then Na2CO3 added to bring pH to 9.5. Colours observed: pale pink, pink, dark pink, colourless, colourless (same immediately and after 5 more minutes). Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1 Na2CO3 solution inhibited the activity of the phosphatase enzyme.
2 More substrate was produced in conditions of high acidity compared to low acidity.
3 The optimum pH for this plant phosphatase must be 5.2.

steps.title: The colour did not change further after more
steps.content: The colour did not change further after 5 more minutes at pH 9.5, showing that adding Na2CO3 (raising pH to an extreme) halted further phosphatase activity — this supports statement 1.

steps.title: Statement is false as worded enzymes convert substrate
steps.content: Statement 2 is false as worded (enzymes convert substrate to product, not the reverse).

steps.title: Statement overreaches with only discrete pH values tested
steps.content: Statement 3 overreaches: with only 5 discrete pH values tested, the darkest tube (pH 5.2) shows the highest activity among those TESTED, but the true optimum could lie at an untested nearby pH, so it cannot be concluded that it 'must be' 5.2.

subtopic: X-linked recessive inheritance in a family tree
question: A gene found on the X chromosome in humans has two alleles, dominant and recessive. Individuals who have only recessive alleles have a condition that affects their eyesight. [DIAGRAM: family tree showing females without the condition, female carriers, males without the condition, and males with the condition]. If one living skin cell from each member of this family were analysed, how many copies of the recessive allele and how many copies of the dominant allele would be found in total?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: Counting alleles across all family members based on
steps.content: Counting alleles across all family members based on their genotype symbols in the family tree (females: 2 X-alleles each; males: 1 X-allele each), and summing recessive vs dominant alleles per the diagram gives 4 recessive and 12 dominant allele copies in total.

question: The graphs below show the oxygen uptake before, during and after a period of strenuous physical exercise. [DIAGRAM: six graph options (A-F) each showing oxygen uptake vs time across rest-exercise-rest phases, with a different shaded area in each]. Which shaded area correctly represents the additional oxygen taken in to repay the oxygen debt acquired through anaerobic respiration?

options.A: shaded area above resting level during the post-exercise recovery period
options.B: shaded area under the entire curve above the resting baseline, spanning both the exercise AND the full recovery period (from where the curve first rises to where it fully returns to the resting baseline)
options.C: shaded area under the curve above the resting baseline, bounded by a hard vertical left edge at the start of exercise and a hard vertical right edge partway through recovery (cut off before the curve fully returns to the resting baseline) -- covers exercise plus only part of recovery
options.D: a rectangular band spanning the entire time axis (rest-exercise-rest) from the resting baseline down to zero, representing baseline resting oxygen consumption itself rather than the exercise-induced excess
options.E: shaded area during the recovery period only, but extending all the way down to zero (the x-axis) rather than just down to the resting baseline -- i.e. the recovery-debt area from A plus the resting-baseline rectangle beneath it
options.F: two separate rectangular strips at resting-baseline height (down to zero), one during the initial rest period before exercise and one during the final rest period after recovery is complete -- excludes the exercise and recovery hump entirely

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: The oxygen debt is repaid during recovery the
steps.content: The oxygen debt is repaid during recovery: the elevated oxygen uptake above the normal resting level, during the recovery period after exercise stops, represents the extra oxygen consumed to repay the debt (e.g., to break down accumulated lactic acid).

subtopic: Investigating temperature effect on plant growth - interpreting results
question:
An investigation used clones of one plant. Twenty plants of the same initial height were divided into four equal groups, each kept at a different temperature but with equal soil/nutrients, equal water, and the same light intensity, grown for six months. [DIAGRAM: bar chart of mean plant height vs temperature for pots 1-4, showing pot 3 and pot 4 with equal mean height]. Which of the following statements could explain the results?
1 The difference in mean height between plants in pot 1 and plants in pot 2 could be due to the environment.
2 The mean height of plants in pot 4 equals that of plants in pot 3 because of another environmental factor in addition to temperature.
3 The mean height of plants in pot 3 equals that of plants in pot 4 because of the genotype of the plants.

steps.title: Since temperature differs between pots the only deliberately
steps.content: Since temperature differs between pots (the only deliberately varied environmental factor other than possibly incidental ones), differences between pot 1 and pot 2 could be environmental (1 plausible).

steps.title: Since the plants are clones identical genotype the
steps.content: Since the plants are clones (identical genotype), the equal heights of pots 3 and 4 could be explained either by temperature reaching a plateau where growth is optimal/limited by another factor (2 plausible) or simply because identical genotype gives the same growth potential once other factors are equal (3 plausible).

question:
An investigation was carried out on the effect of substrate concentration on an enzyme-controlled reaction. Four substrate concentrations were tested (0.25, 0.50, 1.0, 5.0 mmol dm-3), and product concentration was measured over time (0-120 s). [DIAGRAM: graph of product concentration vs time for four substrate concentrations]. Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1 As substrate concentration increases, the time taken to produce 0.2 arbitrary units of product increases.
2 Doubling the substrate concentration always doubles the rate of the reaction.
3 The average rate of reaction for a substrate concentration of 1.0 mmol dm-3 is 0.005 arbitrary units per second.

steps.title: Higher substrate concentration typically decreases not in
steps.content: Higher substrate concentration typically decreases (not increases) the time to reach a given product level, so statement 1 is false.

steps.title: Rate does not keep doubling indefinitely with substrate
steps.content: Rate does not keep doubling indefinitely with substrate concentration since the enzyme becomes saturated (statement 2 false).

steps.title: Reading the graph at mmol dm the reaction
steps.content: Reading the graph, at 1.0 mmol dm-3 the reaction reaches a given product level consistent with an average rate of 0.2/40s = 0.005 arbitrary units per second (statement 3 true, per the graph and answer key).

subtopic: DNA to amino acid sequence translation
question:
The sequence of a coding section of DNA is shown below:
CTAGTCTGGTGGCTACGTCCTCCTCTACTATGGCTAGTCGTCTGGTGGCTA
The number of each type of amino acid coded for by this sequence is: Arg=1, Leu=6, Pro=2, Trp=5, Val=3. What is the correct sequence of amino acids coded for by this sequence of DNA bases?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: Transcribing translating the given DNA coding strand into
steps.content: Transcribing/translating the given DNA coding strand into codons and applying the standard genetic code (with the counts Arg=1, Leu=6, Pro=2, Trp=5, Val=3 as a check on the total of 17 amino acids) yields a specific 17-amino-acid sequence that is the only one consistent with both the codon translation and the given amino acid tally.

subtopic: Hydroelectric power and volume flow rate
question: A hydroelectric power station uses the water in a reservoir to power the generators. The water falls through a vertical height of 150 m to the turbines which power the generators. The efficiency of the power station is 90% and the output power is 1800 MW. (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; density of water = 1000 kg m$^{-3}$) What volume of water passes through the turbines in one minute?

steps.title: Input power kg s
steps.content: Input power $=\dot m g h \Rightarrow \dot m = \frac{2000\times10^6}{10\times150}=1.333\times10^6$ kg/s.

question: It is given that $7\cos x + \tan x \sin x = 5$ where $0° < x < 90°$. What are the possible values of $\tan x$?

steps.title: Set up the equation
steps.content: $\tan x \sin x = \frac{\sin^2 x}{\cos x}$, so $7\cos x + \frac{\sin^2x}{\cos x}=5$.

question: A metal wire of length 0.50 m has a uniform cross-sectional area of $4.0\times10^{-7}\text{ m}^2$. There is a current of 4.0 A in the wire. What is the potential difference across the ends of the wire? (resistivity = $1.6\times10^{-7}\,\Omega$m)

An equilateral triangle of side 8 cm is drawn so that its vertices lie on the circumference of a circle. [DIAGRAM: equilateral triangle inscribed in circle with three circular segments shaded outside the triangle]. What is the total of the three areas shaded in the diagram, in cm$^2$?

A uniform square trap door of side 0.80 m and mass 14 kg has a smooth hinge at one edge and is held open at an angle of 30° to the horizontal. It is supported by a single rigid rod placed so that it meets the surface of the trap door at 90°, at a distance 0.10 m from the top edge of the trap door. [DIAGRAM: trapdoor at 30° to horizontal, hinge at bottom, supporting rod perpendicular to door surface 0.10 m from top edge]. What is the normal contact force exerted on the trap door by the rod? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$)

steps.title: Taking moments about the hinge weight N acts
steps.content: Taking moments about the hinge: weight (140 N) acts at the door's centre, moment arm along the door = 0.40 m, with a perpendicular lever arm of 0.40cos30 deg (since the weight is vertical while the door is inclined).

steps.title: The rod is perpendicular to the door at
steps.content: The rod is perpendicular to the door at 0.80-0.10=0.70 m from the hinge, so its full length is the lever arm for the rod's force.

steps.title: Balancing moments F x x x cos deg
steps.content: Balancing moments: F x 0.70 = 140 x 0.40 x cos30 deg = 28*sqrt(3), so F = 28*sqrt(3)/0.70 = 40*sqrt(3) N.

question: Which one of the following is the real solution of the equation $3\times5^{2x+1}-5^{x}-2=0$?

steps.title: Discriminant so giving rejecting the negative root
steps.content: Discriminant $=1+120=121$, so $u=\dfrac{1\pm11}{30}$, giving $u=\dfrac25$ (rejecting the negative root).

question: Two small loudspeakers are placed side by side 30 cm apart. They are connected to the same signal generator so that they emit sound of frequency 400 Hz in phase with one another. The sounds both reach a microphone placed 40 cm directly in front of one of the two loudspeakers. [DIAGRAM: two speakers 30 cm apart, microphone 40 cm from the nearer speaker]. What is the phase difference between waves from the loudspeakers as they arrive at the microphone? (speed of sound = 320 m/s)

steps.title: Path from the far speaker cm
steps.content: Path from the far speaker: $\sqrt{40^2+30^2}=\sqrt{2500}=50$ cm.

subtopic: Integral defining a function, coefficient in expansion
question: Given that $f(x) = \int_0^x (3+2t)^7\,dt$, what is the coefficient of $x^4$ in the expansion of f(x) in powers of x?

steps.title: Integrating term by term so the coefficient of
steps.content: Integrating term by term, $\int_0^x t^k\,dt=\dfrac{x^{k+1}}{k+1}$, so the coefficient of $x^4$ comes from $k=3$: $\dfrac{1}{4}\binom{7}{3}3^{4}2^{3}=\dfrac14 \times 35 \times 81 \times 8 = 5670$.

question: A student has one 300 Ω resistor and another resistor of resistance R. The student plots a graph of current I against potential difference V for the 300 Ω resistor and then for both resistors connected in parallel. [DIAGRAM: I-V graph, V axis 0-6 V, I axis 0-30 mA, two lines shown]. What is the resistance R?

steps.title: From the graph at V the parallel combination
steps.content: From the graph, at $V=6$ V the parallel combination gives $one=30$ mA, so $R_{parallel}=6/0.03=200\,\Omega$.

question: The three internal angles in a triangle are $\alpha$, $\beta$ and $\theta$, and $3\tan\alpha - 2\sin\beta = 2$, $5\tan\alpha + 6\sin\beta = 8$. What is the value of $\theta$ in degrees?

A light, vertical, copper wire of length 2.4 m and uniform cross-sectional area $2.0\times10^{-6}\text{ m}^2$ supports a load of mass 4.0 kg. The Young modulus of copper is $1.2\times10^{11}$ Pa. What is the strain energy in the wire? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; assume Hooke's law and constant cross-sectional area)
Find the complete set of values of x for which $x^3 - 2x^2 - 7x - 4 > 0$.

steps.title: Since always the sign is determined by except
steps.content: Since $(x+1)^2\geq0$ always, the sign is determined by $(x-4)$ except at $x=-1$ (where the expression equals 0, not $>0$).

steps.title: The expression is positive only when
steps.content: The expression is positive only when $x>4$.

subtopic: Contact force in an accelerating lift
question: The velocity-time graph is for an 80 kg person in a lift that is moving vertically upwards. [DIAGRAM: velocity-time graph, velocity axis 0-2.0 m/s, time axis 0-25 s, with point X marked on the graph]. What is the magnitude of the contact force between the person and the lift floor at the time corresponding to X? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$)

steps.title: Reading the gradient acceleration at point X from
steps.content: Reading the gradient (acceleration) at point X from the graph gives a deceleration while moving upward (or a downward acceleration) of magnitude $a=0.4\text{ m/s}^2$.

steps.title: Using Newton s second law taking the direction
steps.content: Using Newton's second law, $N-mg=ma$ (taking the direction of acceleration into account): $N=m(g+a)=80(10-0.4)=768$ N.

question:

subtopic: area of composite shapes with arcs
question:
[DIAGRAM: Square PQRS with side length $x$. M is the midpoint of side PS. A circular arc centred at M is drawn inside the square from S to P (radius x/2). Another circular arc centred at P is drawn inside the square from S to Q (radius x). The shaded region is the area between the two arcs.]
PQRS is a square with side length $x$. M is the midpoint of side PS. A circular arc, with centre M, is drawn inside the square from S to P. Another circular arc, with centre P, is drawn inside the square from S to Q. What is the area of the shaded region in terms of $x$?

steps.title: The arc centred at P radius sweeps a
steps.content: The arc centred at P (radius $x$) sweeps a quarter circle from S to Q: area $=\frac{1}{4}\pi x^2$.

steps.title: The arc centred at M radius sweeps a
steps.content: The arc centred at M (radius $x/2$) sweeps a semicircle from S to P: area $=\frac{1}{2}\pi (x/2)^2=\frac{1}{8}\pi x^2$.

Which two lines are perpendicular?

steps.title: Perpendicular gradients multiply to line line

steps.content: By the law of sines, the circumradius satisfies $2R=\frac{a}{\sin A}=\frac{8}{\sin 60^\circ}$, so $R=\frac{4}{\sin 60^\circ}$.

The number of pairs of winter boots sold on a day is inversely proportional to the cube of the outside temperature on that day, measured in °C. On a day when the outside temperature is 8°C, 250 pairs of boots are sold. The next day, when the outside temperature is $x$°C, the number of pairs of boots sold is 700% more than on the previous day. What is the value of $x$?

steps.title: More means times the original amount
steps.content: 700% more means 8 times the original amount: $8\times250=2000$.

question:
The table shows statistics relating to the test marks of two groups of students.

The results for the two groups of students are combined. What can be deduced about the mean and range of the combined results?

steps.title: The combined range must be at least as
steps.content: The combined range must be at least as large as the largest individual range (21), since the two groups' data may not overlap, so range $\geq21$.

subtopic: squares from two given vertices (side vs diagonal)
question: Two vertices of a square are at (1, 1) and (3, 5). What is the difference between the perimeters of the largest and smallest possible squares that can be drawn with these points as two of their vertices?

steps.title: If this is a side of the square
steps.content: If this is a side of the square (smallest square), perimeter $=4(2\sqrt{5})=8\sqrt{5}$.

steps.title: If this is a diagonal largest square side
steps.content: If this is a diagonal (largest square), side $=\frac{2\sqrt{5}}{\sqrt{2}}=\sqrt{10}$, perimeter $=4\sqrt{10}$.
