question: A resistor has a constant voltage of 9.00 V across it. A total charge of 180 C passes through the resistor in 4.00 minutes. What is the power dissipated in the resistor?

Air is trapped in a cylinder by a piston. The density of the air in the cylinder is $\rho$. The piston is moved so that the pressure of the trapped air increases by 20%. The temperature of the trapped air does not change. What is the new density of the trapped air? (Assume air is an ideal gas.)

steps.title: From and so at constant temperature
steps.content: From $PV=nRT$ and $\rho=m/V$: $\rho=\dfrac{PM}{RT}$, so at constant temperature $\rho \propto P$.

steps.title: A increase in pressure gives a increase in
steps.content: A 20% increase in pressure gives a 20% increase in density: $1.20\rho$.

question: A non-ideal transformer has 100 turns on the primary coil and 25 turns on the secondary coil. It is provided with 3.0 kW of electrical power at a current of 12.5 A. The voltage output is the same as for an ideal transformer, but the current in the output coil is 40 A. What is the efficiency of the transformer?

subtopic: Newton's second law with multiple bodies
question: A car of mass 1400 kg is towing a caravan of mass 1000 kg along a straight horizontal road at constant speed. The driving force from the engine is increased by 3000 N, causing the car and caravan to accelerate. At one moment, the resistive force on the car has increased by 200 N and the resistive force on the caravan has increased by 400 N. What is the acceleration of the car and caravan at this moment?

A star is moving away from a space telescope positioned above the Earth. The star emits light of frequency $f$ and wavelength $\lambda$ at the speed of light $c$. This light travels through a vacuum to the telescope. The frequency, wavelength and speed measured at the telescope are $f_T$, $\lambda_T$ and $c_T$ respectively. How do these compare with $f$, $\lambda$ and $c$?

options.A: $f_T$ equal to f, $\lambda_T$ equal to λ, $c_T$ equal to c
options.B: $f_T$ equal to f, $\lambda_T$ equal to λ, $c_T$ less than c
options.C: $f_T$ equal to f, $\lambda_T$ greater than λ, $c_T$ equal to c
options.D: $f_T$ equal to f, $\lambda_T$ greater than λ, $c_T$ less than c
options.E: $f_T$ less than f, $\lambda_T$ equal to λ, $c_T$ equal to c
options.F: $f_T$ less than f, $\lambda_T$ equal to λ, $c_T$ less than c
options.G: $f_T$ less than f, $\lambda_T$ greater than λ, $c_T$ equal to c
options.H: $f_T$ less than f, $\lambda_T$ greater than λ, $c_T$ less than c

steps.title: Light travels at constant speed in a vacuum
steps.content: Light travels at constant speed $c$ in a vacuum regardless of source motion (postulate of relativity), so $c_T=c$.

steps.title: A receding source causes redshift observed frequency decr
steps.content: A receding source causes redshift: observed frequency decreases ($f_T<f$) and observed wavelength increases ($\lambda_T>\lambda$), consistent with $c=f\lambda$ being preserved.

question:
Q is an element with several isotopes.
The nuclide with mass number $(3x-7)$ and atomic number $x$ contains 6 neutrons more than the nuclide with mass number $(2x+3)$ and atomic number $x$.
Another isotope of Q is the nuclide with mass number $(3x+1)$ and atomic number $x$.
How many neutrons does the nuclide with mass number $(3x+1)$ contain?

steps.title: Given the first has more neutrons than the
steps.content: Given the first has 6 more neutrons than the second: $2x-7=(x+3)+6 \Rightarrow 2x-7=x+9 \Rightarrow x=16$.

steps.title: The third nuclide then has mass number and
steps.content: The third nuclide then has mass number $3(16)+1=49$ and atomic number $16$, so it contains $49-16=33$ neutrons.

question: A light spring has an uncompressed length of 0.10 m. When an object of mass 0.5 kg rests in equilibrium on top of the spring, the length reduces to 0.08 m. What is the energy stored in the spring due to compression? (g = 10 N kg⁻¹; the spring obeys Hooke's law)

A set of decorative lights consists of 20 lamps connected in series to a dc supply of constant voltage. The total power transferred by all lamps is P. If one lamp fails it becomes short-circuited (zero resistance); the remaining lamps stay lit. What is the new total power transferred by the remaining 19 lamps? (Assume each functioning lamp's resistance is constant.)

steps.title: Originally with identical lamps of resistance each
steps.content: Originally, with 20 identical lamps of resistance $R$ each: $P=\dfrac{V^2}{20R}$.

question: [DIAGRAM: Momentum-time graph for an accelerating train, momentum rising linearly from 0 to about 12×10⁶ kg m s⁻¹ over 4 seconds.] A train accelerates from rest with a constant motor force and a constant friction force of 1.8×10⁷ N. What is the force exerted on the train due to its motors?

steps.title: Gradient of momentum time graph net force
steps.content: Gradient of momentum-time graph = net force.

steps.title: Reading the graph momentum rises from to kg
steps.content: Reading the graph, momentum rises from 0 to $12\times10^6$ kg m s⁻¹ over 4 s, giving net force $=\dfrac{12\times10^6}{4}=3.0\times10^6\text{ N}$.

steps.title: Motor force net force friction
steps.content: Motor force $=$ net force $+$ friction $=3.0\times10^6+1.8\times10^7=2.1\times10^7\text{ N}$.

question: A ship travels into a wave travelling in the opposite direction. The ship has horizontal speed 8.0 ms⁻¹; the wave speed is 3.0 ms⁻¹. The front of the ship rises and falls with a period of 8.0 s. What is the wavelength of the wave?

steps.title: Since ship and wave move toward each other
steps.content: Since ship and wave move toward each other, the relative (encounter) speed $=8.0+3.0=11.0\text{ ms}^{-1}$.

steps.title: The observed period on the ship is the
steps.content: The observed period on the ship is the time between successive wave crests passing it: $\lambda=v_{rel}\times T=11.0\times8.0=88\text{ m}$.

subtopic: Series circuit with non-ohmic component (V-I graphs)
question: [DIAGRAM: Circuit with a 6.0V battery, an 8.0Ω resistor, and a filament lamp in series; ammeter reads 0.25A. Six candidate V-I graphs for the lamp are shown, labelled A–F.] Which graph is a possible V–I graph for the filament lamp?

options.A: Passes through (0.25 A, 2.0 V); curve bends upward with increasing slope (typical filament-lamp shape, resistance increases with current)
options.B: Passes through (0.25 A, 4.0 V); curve bends upward with increasing slope (typical filament-lamp shape, resistance increases with current)
options.C: Passes through (0.25 A, 6.0 V); curve bends upward with increasing slope (typical filament-lamp shape, resistance increases with current)
options.D: Passes through (0.25 A, 2.0 V); curve rises steeply then levels off (decreasing slope, resistance would decrease with current)
options.E: Passes through (0.25 A, 4.0 V); curve rises steeply then levels off (decreasing slope, resistance would decrease with current)
options.F: Passes through (0.25 A, 6.0 V); curve rises steeply then levels off (decreasing slope, resistance would decrease with current)

steps.title: Voltage across lamp at so the graph must
steps.content: Voltage across lamp $=6.0-2.0=4.0\text{ V}$ at $one=0.25\text{ A}$, so the graph must pass through $(0.25\text{ A}, 4.0\text{ V})$.

steps.title: A filament lamp s resistance INCREASES with current
steps.content: A filament lamp\'s resistance INCREASES with current/temperature, so its V-one graph must bend upward with increasing slope, not level off with decreasing slope (which would indicate falling resistance).

steps.title: So the correct curve passes through A V
steps.content: So the correct curve passes through (0.25A, 4.0V) and bends upward with increasing slope.

question: A uniform horizontal magnetic field (0.60 T, west to east) is at 90° to a horizontal wire placed north-south. The wire carries a constant current, has length 0.40 m and mass 0.018 kg. The resultant vertical force on the wire is zero. What are the magnitude and direction of the current? (g = 10 N kg⁻¹)

steps.title: For vertical equilibrium the magnetic force must equal
steps.content: For vertical equilibrium, the magnetic force must equal weight: $BIL=mg \Rightarrow one=\dfrac{mg}{BL}=\dfrac{0.018\times10}{0.60\times0.40}=0.75\text{ A}$.

steps.title: Using Fleming s left hand rule with field
steps.content: Using Fleming's left-hand rule with field west→east and required force upward, the current must flow from north to south.

question: The wavelength range of visible light is 400-700 nm. Light with frequency 6.0×10¹⁴ Hz is green. Microwaves used in cooking have wavelength 12 cm. Which statements are correct? 1: Light with frequency 7.5×10¹⁴ Hz is red. 2: Microwaves used in cooking have frequency 2.5×10⁹ Hz. 3: EM radiation with frequency 2.5×10¹⁵ Hz can be used in thermal imaging of a building. (speed of light = 3.0×10⁸ ms⁻¹)

steps.title: Statement which is violet not red FALSE
steps.content: Statement 1: $\lambda=\dfrac{c}{f}=\dfrac{3.0\times10^8}{7.5\times10^{14}}=400\text{ nm}$, which is violet, not red — FALSE.

steps.title: Statement which is UV not infrared thermal imaging
steps.content: Statement 3: $\lambda=\dfrac{3.0\times10^8}{2.5\times10^{15}}=120\text{ nm}$, which is UV, not infrared (thermal imaging uses IR) — FALSE.

subtopic: Kinematics: uniform acceleration/deceleration with constant speed phase
question: A high-speed straight railway line runs between two cities 60 km apart. The train stops at both cities, accelerates at 1.5 ms⁻² to a maximum speed of 120 ms⁻¹, and decelerates at 2.0 ms⁻² when braking. What is the minimum time taken to travel from one city to the other?

steps.title: Remaining constant speed distance taking
steps.content: Remaining constant-speed distance $=60000-4800-3600=51600\text{ m}$, taking $t_2=\dfrac{51600}{120}=430\text{ s}$.

subtopic: Specific heat capacity from a heating graph
question: [DIAGRAM: Graph of change in temperature ΔT against time t for a metal block of mass M heated at constant rate P, showing a straight line with gradient k.] Which expression gives the specific heat capacity of the metal? (Assume no heat lost.)

steps.title: Set up the equation
steps.content: $Q=Pt$ and $Q=Mc\Delta T$, so $Pt=Mc\Delta T \Rightarrow c=\dfrac{Pt}{M\Delta T}$.

steps.title: Since gradient it follows that so
steps.content: Since gradient $k=\dfrac{\Delta T}{t}$, it follows that $\dfrac{t}{\Delta T}=\dfrac1k$, so $c=\dfrac{P}{Mk}$.

question: A skydiver of mass 80 kg is accelerating vertically downwards through the air. At one instant the skydiver has speed 5.0 ms⁻¹. After a further 20 m downward, the speed has increased to 10 ms⁻¹. What is the average force of air resistance over the 20 m? (g = 10 N kg⁻¹)

A radioactive nuclide X decays in a single stage to stable nuclide R (half-life T years). A radioactive nuclide Y decays in a single stage to stable nuclide S (half-life 2T years). When a rock formed it contained equal numbers of atoms of X, Y, R and S. What is the ratio (number of atoms of R):(number of atoms of S) at time 4T years after formation? (Assume no other processes occur.)

steps.title: The rock formed with EQUAL numbers of X
steps.content: The rock formed with EQUAL numbers ($N_0$) of X, Y, R and S already present, so the totals of R and S must include these initial amounts, not just newly-decayed atoms.

steps.title: At X remaining so newly decayed added to
steps.content: At $t=4T$: X remaining $=N_0(1/2)^4=N_0/16$, so newly-decayed (added to R) $=15N_0/16$; total R $=N_0+15N_0/16=31N_0/16$.

steps.title: Y remaining newly decayed added to S total
steps.content: Y remaining $=N_0(1/2)^2=N_0/4$, newly-decayed (added to S) $=3N_0/4$; total S $=N_0+3N_0/4=7N_0/4$.

subtopic: Latent heat and specific heat capacity (mixtures)
question: A beaker containing 180 g of water at 25°C has a 20 g ice cube at 0°C added to it. No heat is transferred to/from the surroundings. What is the final temperature after all the ice has melted? (specific heat capacity of water = 4 J g⁻¹ °C⁻¹; specific latent heat of fusion of water = 300 J g⁻¹)

steps.title: Energy balance heat lost by warm water heat
steps.content: Energy balance: heat lost by warm water = heat to melt ice + heat to warm melted ice to $T_f$.

question: Liquid X has density 0.80 g cm⁻³ and liquid Y has density 1.0 g cm⁻³. 80 cm³ of X and 100 cm³ of Y are poured into a cylindrical container (cross-sectional area 20 cm²) and allowed to settle without mixing. What is the pressure due to the liquids at a height of 4.0 cm above the interior base? (g = 10 N kg⁻¹)

steps.title: Height of Y denser at bottom height of
steps.content: Height of Y (denser, at bottom) $=\dfrac{100}{20}=5\text{ cm}$; height of X (on top) $=\dfrac{80}{20}=4\text{ cm}$, so Y occupies 0-5 cm and X occupies 5-9 cm.

steps.title: At height cm within Y layer the liquid
steps.content: At height 4 cm (within Y layer), the liquid column above consists of 1 cm of Y plus all 4 cm of X. $P=\rho_Y g h_Y+\rho_X g h_X=1000\times10\times0.01+800\times10\times0.04=100+320=420\text{ Pa}$.

question: A pulse of ultrasound travels from one end of a solid uniform rod of length L, starting at $t=0$. The pulse is partially reflected by a crack in the rod and partially by the far end. The two reflected pulses arrive back at the start at times $t_1$ and $t_2$ ($t_2>t_1$). What is the distance between the crack and the far end of the rod?

steps.title: Speed of sound in rod from the far
steps.content: Speed of sound in rod: from the far-end reflection, $2L=vt_2 \Rightarrow v=\dfrac{2L}{t_2}$.

steps.title: Distance from crack to far end
steps.content: Distance from crack to far end $=L-d=L-\dfrac{Lt_1}{t_2}=\dfrac{L(t_2-t_1)}{t_2}$.

question: The following equations represent reactions of four metals M, Q, R and T: M(s)+HCl(aq)→no reaction; R(s)+TSO4(aq)→RSO4(aq)+T(s); M(s)+QNO3(aq)→MNO3(aq)+Q(s); T(s)+2HCl(aq)→TCl2(aq)+H2(g). Which option lists the order of reactivity from most to least reactive?

steps.title: Order from displacement reactions
steps.content: R displaces T from solution, so R is more reactive than T. T reacts with HCl (produces $\mathrm{H_2}$), so T is more reactive than hydrogen. M does not react with HCl, so M is less reactive than hydrogen.

steps.title: Place M relative to Q
steps.content: M displaces Q from solution, so M is more reactive than Q.

steps.title: Combine the inequalities
steps.content: Overall: R $> $ T $> $ hydrogen $> $ M $> $ Q, so the order is R, T, M, Q.

question: Consider three ions of calcium observed in mass spectrometry: ⁴⁰Ca²⁺, ⁴²Ca²⁺, ⁴³Ca⁺. Which statements are correct? 1: All three ions have electron configuration 2,8,8. 2: ⁴²Ca²⁺ has more neutrons than ⁴⁰Ca²⁺. 3: ⁴²Ca²⁺ has more protons than ⁴³Ca⁺.

steps.title: Ca protons electrons neutrons Ca protons electrons neutrons
steps.content: ⁴⁰Ca^2⁺: 20 protons, 18 electrons (2,8,8), 20 neutrons. ⁴^2Ca^2⁺: 20 protons, 18 electrons (2,8,8), 22 neutrons. ⁴^3Ca⁺: 20 protons, 19 electrons (2,8,8,1), 23 neutrons.

steps.title: Statement is false Ca has electrons config not
steps.content: Statement 1 is false (⁴^3Ca⁺ has 19 electrons, config 2,8,8,1, not 2,8,8).

steps.title: Statement is false both have protons same element
steps.content: Statement 3 is false (both have 20 protons — same element, equal protons).

subtopic: Relative atomic mass from isotopic abundance
question: The relative isotopic abundances of a sample of magnesium are: ²⁴Mg 80%, ²⁵Mg 10%, ²⁶Mg 10%. What is the relative atomic mass (Ar) of the magnesium?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

subtopic: Periodic trends: boiling point, conductivity, reactivity, melting point, bonding
question: A portion of the Periodic Table is given (H; Li, C, O, F; Na, Si, Cl; K, Br). Which one of these trends is correct? A: Boiling point K > Na > Li. B: Electrical conductivity NaCl(l) > NaCl(s) > Na(s). C: Reactivity Br2 > Cl2 > F2. D: Melting point SiO2 > H2O > Na2O. E: Number of double bonds per molecule CO2 > O2 > H2O.

options.E: Number of double bonds per molecule: CO2 > O2 > H2O

steps.title: A is false Group boiling points decrease down
steps.content: A is false (Group 1 boiling points decrease down the group: Li>Na>K).

steps.title: B is false solid Na conducts as a
steps.content: B is false (solid Na conducts as a metal; solid NaCl does not conduct).

steps.title: C is false Group reactivity decreases down the
steps.content: C is false (Group 7 reactivity decreases down the group: F2>Cl2>Br2).

steps.title: D is false order should be SiO giant
steps.content: D is false (order should be SiO2 (giant covalent, ~1710^\circ\mathrm{C}) > Na2O (ionic, ~1132^\circ\mathrm{C}) > H2O (~0^\circ\mathrm{C}), not with H2O second).

steps.title: E is true CO has C O double
steps.content: E is true: CO2 has 2 C=O double bonds, O2 has 1 O=O double bond, H2O has 0 double bonds, so CO2 > O2 > H2O.

question: [DIAGRAM: Graph of temperature vs volume of 1.0 mol dm⁻³ HCl added to 50 cm³ NaOH(aq) in an insulated flask, showing temperature rising then changing slope at volume x cm³.] Which statement explains the shape of the graph?

options.A: The reaction has reached a state of equilibrium.
options.B: An endothermic reaction occurs after x cm³ of hydrochloric acid is added.
options.C: The reaction rate decreases as the acid is used up.
options.D: The sodium hydroxide has been neutralised by x cm³ hydrochloric acid.
options.E: The sodium hydroxide becomes a weaker base as the volume of the resulting solution increases.

steps.title: The exothermic neutralization reaction causes temperature
steps.content: The exothermic neutralization reaction causes temperature to rise as acid is added, until all NaOH is consumed at volume x cm^3 (the equivalence point), after which further acid addition simply dilutes/cools the mixture with no further reaction, causing the change in slope.

steps.title: This is the standard shape for a titration
steps.content: This is the standard shape for a titration temperature curve.

question: [DIAGRAM: Six candidate structural formulae A–F for the repeating unit of an addition polymer.] Which one represents the repeating unit of poly(pent-2-ene)?

options.E: [structure: -CH(CH3)-CH(CH2CH3)- 2-carbon backbone, methyl on one carbon and ethyl on the other]

steps.title: Pent ene is CH CH CH CH CH
steps.content: Pent-2-ene is CH3-CH=CH-CH2-CH3 (double bond between C2 and C3).

steps.title: Addition polymerisation opens the C C bond so
steps.content: Addition polymerisation opens the C2=C3 bond, so the repeating unit retains both original substituents on the two backbone carbons: a methyl group (from C1) on one carbon and an ethyl group (from C4-C5) on the other, giving -CH(CH3)-CH(CH2CH3)-.

question: X is a gaseous element that reacts explosively with hydrogen to produce a single product. Dissolved in water, this forms acidic solution Y. Adding aqueous silver nitrate to Y forms a white precipitate. Y reacts with substance Z to form two products only, one of which forms a white precipitate with aqueous sodium hydroxide. Which of the following could be X and Z?

steps.title: Cl reacts explosively with H to form HCl
steps.content: Cl2 reacts explosively with H2 to form HCl, which dissolves to give hydrochloric acid Y.

steps.title: HCl reacting with Mg Z gives exactly two
steps.content: HCl reacting with Mg (Z) gives exactly two products: MgCl2 + H2.

steps.title: Adding NaOH to MgCl gives Mg OH a
steps.content: Adding NaOH to MgCl2 gives Mg(OH)2, a white precipitate ✓.

question: Some students were trying to assign oxidation numbers to each of the four sulfur atoms in the tetrathionate ion, S4O6²⁻. Which list gives the possible oxidation states of the four sulfur atoms present?

steps.title: In the tetrathionate ion S O the structure
steps.content: In the tetrathionate ion S4O6^2⁻, the structure has a central S-S bond (two sulfur atoms at oxidation state 0) flanked by two terminal SO3 groups (sulfur at oxidation state +5).

steps.title: This gives oxidation states and the overall charge
steps.content: This gives oxidation states 0, 0, +5, +5, and the overall charge check: $2(0)+2(+5)+6(-2)=10-12=-2$ ✓.

question: The table shows reagents in three organic reactions. Which correctly show(s) the product(s)? 1: CH3CH=CH2 + HBr → 1,2-dibromopropane (only). 2: C2H5COOH + CH3OH (H+ catalyst) → methyl propanoate and water. 3: C2H5OH + Na → sodium ethanoate and hydrogen.

steps.title: Statement is false HBr adds across the double
steps.content: Statement 1 is false: HBr adds across the double bond to give 2-bromopropane (via Markovnikov addition), not 1,2-dibromopropane (which would require Br2).

steps.title: Statement is true this is Fischer esterification giving
steps.content: Statement 2 is true: this is Fischer esterification, giving methyl propanoate + water.

steps.title: Statement is false ethanol sodium gives sodium ethoxide
steps.content: Statement 3 is false: ethanol + sodium gives sodium ethoxide (C2H5ONa) + hydrogen, not sodium ethanoate (which requires the carboxylic acid).

question: Chlorine gas reacts with hot concentrated aqueous sodium hydroxide to form sodium chloride, sodium chlorate(V) and water. The unbalanced ionic equation is: $u\text{Cl}_2+v\text{OH}^-\to w\text{Cl}^-+x\text{ClO}_3^-+y\text{H}_2\text{O}$. What is the simplest ratio of $w:x$ in the balanced equation?

steps.title: This is a disproportionation of Cl oxidation state
steps.content: This is a disproportionation of Cl2 (oxidation state 0) to Cl⁻ (-1, reduction) and ClO3⁻ (+5, oxidation).

steps.title: Balancing electrons each Cl formed involves gain of
steps.content: Balancing electrons: each Cl⁻ formed involves gain of 1 electron; each ClO3⁻ formed involves loss of 5 electrons.

steps.title: For balance Cl are formed for every ClO
steps.content: For balance, 5 Cl⁻ are formed for every 1 ClO3⁻ formed (5\times1 = 1\times5 electrons).

subtopic: Two-way paper chromatography and Rf values
question: [DIAGRAM: Two-way chromatogram showing final positions of four amino acids after development in solvent 1 then solvent 2 (rotated 90°).] Which of the following statements is correct?

options.A: Leucine travels further relative to the solvent front in solvent 2 than in solvent 1.
options.B: Lysine has a greater Rf value in solvent 1 than it has in solvent 2.
options.C: Solvent 1 alone could be used to separate all four amino acids.
options.D: Solvent 2 alone could be used to separate all four amino acids.
options.E: The Rf value of tyrosine in solvent 1 is 0.6 and in solvent 2 is 0.7.

steps.title: From the chromatogram solvent front cm solvent front
steps.content: From the chromatogram: solvent-1 front=10cm, solvent-2 front=20cm.

steps.title: Lysine distance in solvent cm Rf distance in
steps.content: Lysine: distance in solvent1=6cm -> Rf1=0.6; distance in solvent2=8cm -> Rf2=0.4.

steps.title: Since lysine s Rf is greater in solvent
steps.content: Since 0.6>0.4, lysine's Rf is greater in solvent 1. (Checking the others: glycine and leucine share the same Rf1=0.8, so solvent1 alone cannot separate all four; glycine and tyrosine share the same Rf2=0.3, so solvent2 alone cannot separate all four; tyrosine's actual Rf2 is 0.3, not 0.7.)

question: A reaction between copper and nitric acid produces a blue solution of copper(II) nitrate, water and substance X only. X does not contain copper or hydrogen. The balanced equation shows 1 mole of copper reacts to produce 2 moles of water. What is the identity of substance X?

steps.title: Hydrogen balance H on left H in water
steps.content: Hydrogen balance: 4H on left = 2\times2H in water on right ✓ (matches given 2 mol water).

steps.title: Nitrogen balance N on left Cu NO accounts
steps.content: Nitrogen balance: 4N on left; Cu(NO3)2 accounts for 2N, so X must account for 2N total (1N per X if 2 mol X).

steps.title: Oxygen balance left has O Cu NO has
steps.content: Oxygen balance: left has 12 O; Cu(NO3)2 has 6 O, water has 2 O, remaining 4 O split over 2 mol X = 2 O each.

steps.title: So X NO N O giving X NO
steps.content: So X = NO2 (1 N, 2 O), giving X = NO2 (nitrogen dioxide, from reaction with concentrated nitric acid).

question: Ethanedioic acid, (COOH)2, is a weak diprotic acid. What is the minimum volume of a 2.50 mol dm⁻³ solution of ethanedioic acid required to neutralise 25.0 cm³ of 2.00 mol dm⁻³ sodium hydroxide solution?

subtopic: Heat released by combustion and temperature change
question: Propene burns in air, releasing 2000 kJ per mole burned. 2.10 g of propene is burned to heat a 1000 g sample of olive oil, initially at 23.0°C. It takes 2.00 J to heat 1 g of olive oil by 1.0°C. Assume all heat is transferred to the oil. What is the maximum temperature reached? (Mr: C3H6 = 42.0)
What is the calculated energy change for CH4(g)+2H2O(g)→CO2(g)+4H2(g) using bond energies: H-H 440, O-H 460, C-H 430, C-O 360, C=O 800, C≡O 1070 (kJ mol⁻¹)?

steps.title: Bonds broken C H in CH O H
Bonds formed C O in CO H H
Energy change bonds broken bonds formed endothermic
steps.content: Energy change $=$ bonds broken $-$ bonds formed $=3560-3360=+200\text{ kJ mol}^{-1}$ (endothermic).

subtopic: Limiting reagent and total gas volume
question: The balanced equation for oxidation of ammonia is 4NH3(g)+5O2(g)→4NO(g)+6H2O(g). 50.0 dm³ of ammonia and 50.0 dm³ of oxygen, both at 850°C and 1 atm, are mixed and react fully. No other reactions occur. What is the maximum total volume of gases after the reaction (same conditions)?

steps.title: Available NH O so O is limiting
steps.content: Available: NH3/4 = 12.5, O2/5 = 10, so O2 is limiting.

steps.title: O dm fully reacts with dm NH leaving
steps.content: O2 (50 dm^3) fully reacts with $\tfrac45\times50=40$ dm^3 NH3, leaving $50-40=10$ dm^3 NH3 unreacted.

steps.title: Products NO dm H O dm

question: Electrolysis of molten KCl produces potassium at the negative electrode and chlorine at the positive electrode. Electrolysis of aqueous CuSO4 deposits copper at the negative electrode. Assuming the same number of electrons is transferred in both experiments, arrange the elements in order of mass produced/deposited from lowest to highest. (Ar: Cl=35.5, K=39.0, Cu=63.5)

steps.title: K e K electron per K atom mass
steps.content: K⁺+e⁻→K (1 electron per K atom): mass per mole electrons $=39.0$.

steps.title: Cl Cl e electron per Cl atom mass
steps.content: Cl⁻→½Cl2+e⁻ (1 electron per Cl atom): mass per mole electrons $=35.5$.

steps.title: Cu e Cu electrons per Cu atom mass
steps.content: Cu^2⁺+2e⁻→Cu (2 electrons per Cu atom): mass per mole electrons $=63.5/2=31.75$.

steps.title: For equal charge passed mass order lowest to
steps.content: For equal charge passed, mass order (lowest to highest): copper (31.75) < chlorine (35.5) < potassium (39.0).

subtopic: Reading a reaction-progress graph for remaining reactant mass
question: [DIAGRAM: Graph of total volume of gas released vs time for 0.500 g magnesium (excess) reacting with dilute hydrochloric acid, showing the reaction reaching completion.] What is the mass of magnesium remaining after two seconds? (Ar: Mg=24; 1 mole of gas at RTP = 24.0 dm³)

steps.title: Total Mg which would produce via Mg HCl
steps.content: Total Mg $=0.500\text{ g}=\dfrac{0.500}{24}\text{ mol}$, which would produce (via Mg+2HCl→MgCl2+H2) the same number of moles of H2, giving a total gas volume at completion of $\dfrac{0.500}{24}\times24000=500\text{ cm}^3$.

steps.title: Reading the volume of gas released at from
steps.content: Reading the volume of gas released at $t=2\text{ s}$ from the graph and converting to moles of Mg reacted (1:1 with H2 produced) allows calculation of Mg consumed, and hence Mg remaining $=0.500-\text{Mg reacted}$.

question: A mixture of sodium nitrate and barium bromide solids, combined mass 6.36 g, was dissolved in water. Excess aqueous silver nitrate was added, forming a precipitate, filtered and dried to mass 3.76 g. What was the mass of sodium nitrate in the original mixture? (Mr: NaNO3=85, BaBr2=297, AgBr=188)

steps.title: Only BaBr reacts with AgNO to form AgBr
steps.content: Only BaBr2 reacts with AgNO3 to form AgBr precipitate: $\text{BaBr}_2+2\text{AgNO}_3\to2\text{AgBr}+\text{Ba(NO}_3)_2$.

steps.title: Let mass BaBr moles BaBr moles AgBr mass
steps.content: Let mass BaBr2 $=m$: moles BaBr2 $=\dfrac{m}{297}$, moles AgBr $=\dfrac{2m}{297}$, mass AgBr $=\dfrac{2m}{297}\times188=3.76 \Rightarrow m=\dfrac{3.76\times297}{376}=2.97\text{ g}$.

subtopic: Rate of gas production over time (decreasing rate curve)
question: A spoonful of magnesium carbonate powder was added to excess hydrochloric acid in an open conical flask on an electronic balance: MgCO3(s)+2HCl(aq)→MgCl2(aq)+CO2(g)+H2O(l). Mass was measured initially and at 1-minute intervals; total mass of gas produced was calculated. The reaction stopped at 5 minutes. Which row could represent the total mass of gas calculated after each measurement?

steps.title: As the reaction proceeds MgCO the limiting reagent
steps.content: As the reaction proceeds, MgCO3 (the limiting reagent in excess acid) is used up, so the reaction rate decreases over time -- the mass of gas produced per minute should decrease progressively, giving a curve that rises steeply at first then levels off.

steps.title: The pattern with per minute increments of g
steps.content: The pattern with per-minute increments of 5,4,3,2,1 g shows exactly this decreasing-rate pattern, unlike constant increments or increasing increments.

question: A selection pressure is a biological or physical factor in an environment that may result in evolution. Which situations result in selection pressures on one or more organisms? 1: clearing rainforests to grow palm oil plantations. 2: introduction of a predator to islands with seabird colonies. 3: long-term use of antibiotics in hospital wards. 4: using an insecticide to kill mosquitoes that spread malaria.

steps.title: All four scenarios create a change in environmental
steps.content: All four scenarios create a change in environmental conditions that favours survival/reproduction of certain phenotypes over others: habitat loss (1) selects against species unable to adapt; predator introduction (2) selects for anti-predator traits; antibiotic use (3) selects for resistant bacteria; insecticide use (4) selects for resistant mosquitoes.

question: A particular cell has: a cell wall, a cell membrane, no mitochondria. Which statement about this cell is correct?

options.A: It may be an animal cell.
options.D: It contains X and Y chromosomes.
options.E: It is not able to respire.

steps.title: A cell wall rules out animal cells A
steps.content: A cell wall rules out animal cells (A false).

steps.title: Having no mitochondria doesn t rule out being
steps.content: Having no mitochondria doesn't rule out being prokaryotic (bacteria have cell walls, no mitochondria, and no nucleus) — so it may have no nucleus (B true).

steps.title: Chloroplast containing cells plants still have mitochondr
steps.content: Chloroplast-containing cells (plants) still have mitochondria, so C is inconsistent with no mitochondria being possible for such organisms in general reasoning; a bacterial cell wouldn't have chloroplasts either typically tested this way.

steps.title: D is not implied by the given features
steps.content: D is not implied by the given features.

steps.title: Bacteria can still respire anaerobically without mitochon
steps.content: Bacteria can still respire anaerobically without mitochondria, so E is false.

question: [DIAGRAM: Graph showing concentration of a substance at different distances from a cell membrane, with a steady concentration difference maintained between inside and outside the cell over time.] Which process(es) is/are responsible for maintaining the difference in concentration across the membrane? 1: active transport. 2: diffusion. 3: osmosis.

steps.title: A concentration difference maintained over time despite a
steps.content: A concentration difference maintained over time despite a gradient existing (which would otherwise cause diffusion down the gradient) must be actively sustained against the gradient.

steps.title: Only active transport can move substances against a
steps.content: Only active transport can move substances against a concentration gradient using energy (ATP); diffusion and osmosis act down gradients and would tend to eliminate the difference, not maintain it.

question: [DIAGRAM: Diagram showing glucose in blood plasma interconverting with glycogen in muscles and glycogen in liver via processes 1, 2 and 3.] Which hormones stimulate the processes shown by the arrows?

steps.title: Glucose to glycogen in liver storage is stimulated
steps.content: Glucose to glycogen (in liver, storage) is stimulated by insulin.

steps.title: Glycogen muscle liver to glucose breakdown is stimulated
steps.content: Glycogen (muscle/liver) to glucose (breakdown) is stimulated by glucagon and/or adrenaline depending on the arrow direction shown.

steps.title: Matching the standard blood glucose regulation pathway in
steps.content: Matching the standard blood glucose regulation pathway (insulin promotes glycogenesis; glucagon/adrenaline promote glycogenolysis) to the three labelled arrows: arrow 1 = insulin, arrow 2 = insulin/adrenaline, arrow 3

question: [DIAGRAM: Gametes P and Q fuse to form cell R, which divides by mitosis into cells S and T, which grow into two separate individuals.] Which statements are correct? 1: The number of double strands of DNA is the same in gamete P and cell T. 2: If gamete Q contains a Y chromosome, both individuals from S and T will be genetically male. 3: A mutation in the DNA in cell R before mitosis will always change the phenotype of cell S.

steps.title: Gametes are haploid n while cell T formed
steps.content: Gametes are haploid (n) while cell T (formed by mitosis from diploid R) is diploid (2n), so statement 1 is false (different amounts of DNA/chromosome number).

steps.title: If gamete Q carries a Y chromosome and
steps.content: If gamete Q carries a Y chromosome and fuses with P ( carrying X), R is XY; since S and T arise from mitosis of R, both are genetically identical to R (both XY, i.e. genetically male) — statement 2 is true.

steps.title: A mutation doesn t always change phenotype it
steps.content: A mutation doesn't always change phenotype (it could be silent, in a non-coding region, or recessive) — statement 3 is false.

question: [DIAGRAM: Graph of tortoise population size over the last century on a single Galapagos island, showing a decline after time Z.] Which could account for the change in population shown after time Z? 1: reduced rainfall. 2: reduced availability of resources. 3: failure to adapt to competition from an introduced species.

steps.title: All three factors could plausibly cause a population
steps.content: All three factors could plausibly cause a population decline: reduced rainfall (1) could reduce water/food availability directly affecting survival; reduced resource availability (2) directly limits population via reduced carrying capacity; competition from an introduced species (3) if the tortoises fail to adapt could reduce their access to resources and reduce numbers.

steps.title: All three are valid explanations so H
steps.content: All three are valid explanations, so H.

question: [DIAGRAM: Graph of total mass of product formed vs time (0-70s) for an enzyme-catalysed reaction, showing the curve levelling off.] Which statements are correct? 1: The enzymes may have been used up in the reaction. 2: The initial rate of reaction is 120 g min⁻¹. 3: At high concentrations, the product formed may inhibit the enzymes.

steps.title: Enzymes are biological catalysts and are not consumed
steps.content: Enzymes are biological catalysts and are not consumed/used up in a reaction (they are regenerated), so statement 1 is false.

steps.title: The initial rate would be read as the
steps.content: The initial rate would be read as the gradient of the graph near t=0 in g/s then converted; without a value matching exactly 120 g min⁻¹ from the graph's early gradient, this is not confirmed — statement 2 is false.

steps.title: Product inhibition where accumulated product binds to and
steps.content: Product inhibition (where accumulated product binds to and inhibits the enzyme, e.g. competitive/allosteric inhibition) is a recognised real phenomenon that would explain the levelling off of the curve — statement 3 is true.

question: A person ran on a treadmill for 360 seconds. Rates of aerobic and anaerobic respiration were measured at start and end: aerobic 1.01→5.77, anaerobic 0.01→3.67 (arbitrary units). Which statements are correct during the 360 seconds? 1: There was an increase in pH that caused a change in the shape of the respiratory enzyme's active sites. 2: Part of the increase in rate of cellular respiration may have been due to a temperature increase in the muscles. 3: More carbon dioxide needed to be removed from the muscle cells.

steps.title: Anaerobic respiration produces lactic acid which would DE
steps.content: Anaerobic respiration produces lactic acid, which would DECREASE pH (make it more acidic), not increase it — statement 1 is false.

steps.title: Exercise generates heat from increased metabolic activity
steps.content: Exercise generates heat from increased metabolic activity, and higher temperature increases enzyme activity/reaction rate (up to the optimum) — statement 2 is true.

steps.title: Increased aerobic respiration produces more CO as a
steps.content: Increased aerobic respiration produces more CO2 as a waste product, which must be removed — statement 3 is true.

subtopic: Genetics with lethal homozygous dominant allele
question: Coat colour in a mouse population is controlled by one gene (not sex-linked) with alleles R and r. Heterozygous mice (Rr) have yellow fur. Homozygous dominant (RR) embryos do not survive. A yellow male and yellow female mouse were mated repeatedly; offspring were grey and yellow. Assuming no new mutations, which is correct?

options.A: 25% of the live offspring will be grey in colour.
options.B: All grey mice have a homozygous genotype for coat colour.
options.C: Offspring with XY chromosomes are all heterozygous for coat colour.
options.D: The live offspring of a cross between a yellow and a grey mouse will always be yellow.
options.E: There is a 3:1 ratio of dominant to recessive alleles for this gene in the live offspring.

steps.title: Cross Rr Rr gives genotypes RR Rr rr
steps.content: Cross Rr \times Rr gives genotypes 1 RR : 2 Rr : 1 rr.

steps.title: RR is lethal doesn t survive leaving live
steps.content: RR is lethal (doesn't survive), leaving live offspring in ratio 2 Rr (yellow) : 1 rr (grey), i.e. 2/3 yellow, 1/3 grey (so A's 25% is wrong; correct grey proportion is 33.3%).

steps.title: Grey mice must be homozygous recessive rr since
steps.content: Grey mice must be homozygous recessive (rr), since yellow is exclusively the heterozygous phenotype — so all grey mice are homozygous (rr) for coat colour, making B correct.

steps.title: C is unrelated gene isn t sex linked
D is false yellow Rr grey rr gives
steps.content: D is false: yellow (Rr) \times grey (rr) gives 1 Rr (yellow) : 1 rr (grey), not always yellow.

question: One strand of a section of DNA has the sequence: AATCGGTCTTGCGGCCAAGGCCCTT. The complementary strand is not shown. Which chart shows the correct proportions of the four bases (A, C, G, T) for this section of double-stranded DNA? (Assume no mutations.)

options.A: [pie chart: labels A,C,T,G; wedges A=T (10/50 each), G=C (15/50 each)]
options.B: [pie chart: labels T,A,C,G; wedge proportions differ from A=T=10, G=C=15]
options.C: [pie chart: labels A,G,C,T; A=C smaller wedges, T=G larger wedges]
options.D: [pie chart: labels A,C,T,G; A=C smaller wedges, T=G larger wedges]
options.E: [pie chart: labels A,C,G,T; wedge proportions differ from A=T=10, G=C=15]
options.F: [pie chart: labels A,C / G,T as four equal 25% quadrants]

steps.title: Count bases on the given strand
Apply complementary base pairing
steps.content: In double-stranded DNA, every $\mathrm{A}$ pairs with $\mathrm{T}$ and every $\mathrm{G}$ with $\mathrm{C}$, so totals are $$\mathrm{A}=\mathrm{T}=4+6=10, \qquad \mathrm{G}=\mathrm{C}=7+8=15$$ out of $50$ bases.

steps.title: Identify the matching pie chart
steps.content: Proportions are $\mathrm{A}:\mathrm{T}:\mathrm{G}:\mathrm{C} = 10:10:15:15 = 2:2:3:3$. The correct chart has equal $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{T}$ wedges (each $20\%$) and equal, larger $\mathrm{G}$ and $\mathrm{C}$ wedges (each $30\%$).

question: [DIAGRAM: Two identical plant cells, one placed in concentrated sugar solution and one in distilled water for 2 hours, showing regions Q and S.] Which statements are correct? 1: In the cell in distilled water, Q contains only distilled water. 2: In the cell in concentrated sugar solution, the number of solute particles in Q increased over the two hours. 3: S is a vacuum.

steps.title: Q represents the still cell sap containing vacuole
steps.content: Q represents the (still cell-sap-containing) vacuole/cytoplasm region, not pure distilled water even in the water-treated cell — water moves in by osmosis but the vacuole still contains dissolved solutes (cell sap), so statement 1 is false.

steps.title: In the plasmolysed cell concentrated sugar solution water
steps.content: In the plasmolysed cell (concentrated sugar solution), water moves OUT of the cell by osmosis, so the volume of Q decreases but the number of solute particles inside does not increase (no new solutes are added) — statement 2 is false.

steps.title: S represents the gap between the shrunken protoplast
steps.content: S represents the gap between the shrunken protoplast and the cell wall, which is filled with the external (sugar) solution that has entered, not a vacuum — statement 3 is false.

steps.title: None of the statements are correct
steps.content: None of the statements are correct.

question: [DIAGRAM: Carbon cycle diagram showing plants, animals, bacteria and CO2 in air connected by three labelled multi-stage processes P, Q and R.] Which statements are correct? 1: P requires the presence of mitochondria. 2: Overall, Q releases heat. 3: R is sensitive to changes in pH and temperature.

steps.title: Based on the typical carbon cycle diagram layout
steps.content: Based on the typical carbon cycle diagram layout, P likely represents photosynthesis (which occurs in chloroplasts, not mitochondria — statement 1 false), Q likely represents feeding/consumption relationships between organisms (not inherently a heat-releasing process in itself — statement 2 false), and R represents respiration or decomposition (an enzyme-catalysed process, and enzymes are indeed sensitive to pH and temperature — statement 3 true).

question: Two cells, L and M, were studied using a microscope. Actual maximum length: cell L = 400 μm, cell M = 40 μm. Maximum length in drawing: cell L = 2 cm, cell M = 1 cm. Which statements are correct? 1: Cell L has been magnified 50 times. 2: Cell M has been magnified 5 times as much as cell L. 3: Both cells could have a cell wall.

steps.title: Magnification of L statement true
steps.content: Magnification of L $=\dfrac{2\text{ cm}=20000\ \mu m}{400\ \mu m}=50\times$ — statement 1 true.

steps.title: Magnification of M
steps.content: Magnification of M $=\dfrac{1\text{ cm}=10000\ \mu m}{40\ \mu m}=250\times$.

steps.title: Ratio M L magnification statement true
Both plant and bacterial cells and fungal cells
steps.content: Both plant and bacterial cells (and fungal cells) have cell walls, and cells of these sizes (400 μm could be a plant cell, 40 μm could be a plant or bacterial cell) are plausible — statement 3 true.

question: [DIAGRAM: Pedigree diagram showing inheritance of a recessive-allele phenotypic feature across individuals 1-6, with individual 6's phenotype and sex unknown.] What is the probability that individual 6 is an unaffected male?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: From the pedigree determine the genotypes of individual
steps.content: From the pedigree, determine the genotypes of individual 6's parents based on the affected/unaffected status of individuals 1-5, apply Mendelian ratios for the recessive trait to find P(unaffected) for individual 6, then multiply by P(male) = 1/2 (assuming independent, unlinked sex determination) to get the combined probability, consistent with the key answer of 37.5% (=3/4 \times 1/2).

question: [DIAGRAM: Three diagrams showing daughter cells produced when three different stem cells divide: stem cell 1, stem cell 2, and stem cell 3, each producing different combinations of stem/specialised cells.] Which statement is correct?

options.A: Only stem cell 1 shows division by mitosis.
options.B: Some cancers result from divisions like that shown for stem cell 1.
options.C: The total number of stem cells increases if they divide like stem cell 2.
options.D: Stem cells in adults divide like stem cell 3.
options.E: The total number of stem cells is maintained if they divide like stem cell 3.

steps.title: All types of stem cell division shown occur
steps.content: All types of stem cell division shown occur via mitosis (A is false — mitosis underlies all somatic cell division regardless of daughter cell fate).

steps.title: If stem cell s division pattern involves uncontrolled
steps.content: If stem cell 1's division pattern involves uncontrolled/excessive production of stem cells (self-renewal without proper regulation), this uncontrolled proliferation is analogous to how some cancers arise from unchecked cell division — B is correct given the pattern shown for stem cell 1 in the diagram.

question: A fungus feeds by releasing amylase onto starchy food. The soluble products of the breakdown of starch are absorbed by the fungus. Test tubes were set up containing a mixture of starch solution and fungus. Each test tube was maintained at a different temperature between 5°C and 45°C. Samples of the mixture were removed early in the experiments to determine the initial rates of this enzyme-catalysed reaction. The results were plotted. All of the other variables were kept constant. [DIAGRAM: five graphs of rate of reaction against temperature (5°C to 45°C)] Which graph shows the expected results?

options.A: rate starts high, decreases sharply then levels off low (decay curve)
options.B: rate starts high, decreases to a minimum, then increases again (U-shape)
options.C: rate decreases linearly from high to low across the temperature range
options.D: rate increases from low, then plateaus at a maximum with no decline
options.E: rate increases from low to a peak around the optimum temperature, then declines slightly (bell-shaped)

steps.title: Enzyme catalysed reaction rate increases with temperature
steps.content: Enzyme-catalysed reaction rate increases with temperature (more kinetic energy, more successful collisions) up to an optimum, then declines as the enzyme denatures at higher temperatures.

steps.title: This produces a rise then fall bell shaped
steps.content: This produces a rise-then-fall (bell-shaped) pattern peaking around the optimum temperature.

subtopic: Red blood cell production rate calculation
question: Red blood cells are produced by stem cells in the bone marrow. A 1 mm³ sample of blood from a healthy person contains 4×10⁶ red blood cells. The person has a total blood volume of 0.006 m³, a constant total red blood cell count, and red blood cells have a lifespan of 100 days. Which statements are correct? 1: Red blood cells are phagocytic cells. 2: The average rate of production of red blood cells is 1×10¹⁰ cells per hour. 3: The stem cells that produce red blood cells do not have nuclei.

steps.title: Since count is constant production rate replacement rate
steps.content: Since count is constant, production rate = replacement rate = total/lifespan: $\dfrac{2.4\times10^{13}}{100\text{ days}}=2.4\times10^{11}\text{ cells/day}=\dfrac{2.4\times10^{11}}{24}=1\times10^{10}\text{ cells/hour}$ — statement 2 true.

steps.title: Red blood cells are not phagocytic that s
steps.content: Red blood cells are not phagocytic (that's white blood cells, e.g. neutrophils/macrophages) — statement 1 false.

steps.title: Stem cells unlike mature red blood cells do
steps.content: Stem cells (unlike mature red blood cells) do have nuclei — statement 3 false.

subtopic: Meiosis and haplodiploidy in ant life cycle
question: [DIAGRAM: Life cycle of an ant species where males are haploid and females are diploid: egg, adult male, sperm, zygote, adult female, with stages labelled P, Q, R, S.] Which of the letters represent(s) meiosis?

steps.title: In haplodiploid species males develop from unfertilised h
steps.content: In haplodiploid species, males develop from unfertilised (haploid) eggs via mitosis only (no meiosis needed, since the egg is already haploid), while females develop from fertilised (diploid) eggs.

steps.title: Meiosis occurs specifically in the diploid female when
steps.content: Meiosis occurs specifically in the diploid female when producing haploid eggs (halving chromosome number from diploid to haploid).

steps.title: Only the process producing haploid gametes from the
steps.content: Only the process producing haploid gametes from the diploid female (labelled P) represents meiosis.

subtopic: Genome, alleles and differentiation across cell types
question: A student compared the properties of different cells from one healthy human. Which statements are correct? (Assume no mutations.) 1: A cheek cell contains the same alleles as an embryonic stem cell. 2: A sperm cell contains the same genome as a cheek cell. 3: A white blood cell contains the same number of DNA bases as a mature red blood cell. 4: An embryonic stem cell produces all of the same proteins as a white blood cell.

steps.title: All somatic diploid cells in one person including
steps.content: All somatic (diploid) cells in one person, including cheek cells and embryonic stem cells, share the same genome/alleles (barring mutation) — statement 1 is true.

steps.title: Sperm cells are haploid so they contain half
steps.content: Sperm cells are haploid, so they contain half the genome/chromosome number of a diploid cheek cell, not the same genome — statement 2 is false.

steps.title: Mature red blood cells in mammals lose their
steps.content: Mature red blood cells (in mammals) lose their nucleus and hence contain no DNA, unlike white blood cells — statement 3 is false.

steps.title: Different cell types express different genes differential
steps.content: Different cell types express different genes (differential gene expression), so an embryonic stem cell does not produce all the same proteins as a specialised white blood cell — statement 4 is false.

question: [DIAGRAM: Kite diagram showing distribution of dandelions and daisies along a 30m transect in a field, sampled using a 0.5m-sided quadrat. Each square on the vertical axis = 1 plant; e.g. the quadrat centred at 5m had 6 daisies.] Which statements about the data are/is correct? 1: Across the transect, the number of dandelions is proportional to the number of daisies. 2: Repeating the experiment along a different transect would result in an identical pattern. 3: The density of dandelions at 5m is 36 plants per square metre.

steps.title: Statement requires a consistent proportional relationship
steps.content: Statement 1 requires a consistent proportional relationship between dandelion and daisy counts at every point, which is not generally true from raw kite diagram data (they can vary independently) — false.

steps.title: Statement natural variation across a field means different
steps.content: Statement 2: natural variation across a field means different transects would give different (not identical) patterns — false.

steps.title: If the dandelion count at m was say
steps.content: If the dandelion count at 5m was, say, 9 plants (given the example daisies=6 at the same point uses similar reading), density $=\dfrac{9}{0.25}=36$ plants per m^2 — this matches, so statement 3 is true.

question: Which one of the following is a simplification of $y\left(\dfrac{3x^{1/2}z}{y^3}\right)^2$?

subtopic: Pythagoras with a point dividing a side
question: Triangle PQR has a right angle at Q. The point T lies on QR such that QT = (1/4)QR. PT = 6 cm and PR = 12 cm. What is the length of QT, in cm?

steps.title: Since angle Q is right and
steps.content: Since angle Q is right, $h^2+QT^2=PT^2=36$ and $h^2+QR^2=PR^2=144$.

question: Find the complete set of values of $x$ that satisfy the inequality: $\dfrac{3}{4}(5-x) - \dfrac{1}{2}(6-x) - x < 0$

I have two fair dice, X and Y, each with six sides. The faces on X are labelled 1, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. The faces on Y are labelled 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 6. I roll the dice together and calculate my total score by adding the number rolled on X to the number rolled on Y. What is the probability that my total score is greater than 9?

steps.title: Sum means sum or max is
Listing X Y ways giving X Y way
steps.content: Listing: X=4,Y=6(\times2)=2 ways giving 10; X=5,Y=5=1 way giving10; X=5,Y=6(\times2)=2 ways giving 11.

question: Rob keeps a record of what he earns each day. On Monday, he earned 50% less than he earned on Sunday. On Tuesday, he earned 20% more than he earned on Monday. On Wednesday, he earned 30% less than he earned on Tuesday. On Wednesday, he earned £84. How much did Rob earn on Sunday?

steps.title: Wednesday Tuesday Tuesday Monday Monday Sunday

question: The nth term of a sequence T is $(n-3)^2$, where $n$ is a positive integer. The nth term of another sequence V is $3n+p$, where $p$ is a constant and $n$ is a positive integer. The 10th term in T is equal to twice the 5th term in V. What is the 4th term in V?

question: Which one of the following is a simplification of $\dfrac{5x^2-17x-12}{25x^2-9} \div \dfrac{x^2+x-12}{x^2-x-6}$?

question: S is a list of six numbers: 1, 2, $x$, $x+1$, $x+1$, 15, where $2 \le x \le 14$. The mean of S is one more than the median of S. What is the value of $x$?

steps.title: With the list in order is
steps.content: With $2\le x\le14$ the list in order is $1,2,x,x+1,x+1,15$.

question: A rectangle PQRS has length $(2x-1)$ cm and width $(x+1)$ cm. A larger rectangle is made by adding 3 cm to both the length and the width of PQRS. The larger rectangle has an area of 360 cm². What is the ratio of PQ to PS?

steps.title: Then ratio simplifies to i e as labelled
steps.content: Then $PQ=2(11)-1=21$, $PS=11+1=12$; ratio $21:12$ simplifies to $7:4$, i.e. $PS:PQ=4:7$ as labelled on the diagram.

question: $t$ is inversely proportional to the square of $w$. $t$ and $w$ are positive numbers. $t=36$ when $w=2\times10^{-2}$. What is the value of $w$ when $t=100$?
PQRS is a trapezium [DIAGRAM: trapezium PQRS with angle P=30 degrees, QR=40 m, and tan(angle RSQ)=5/8]. What is the length of PS, in metres?

steps.title: Begin from the given information and apply the
steps.content: Begin from the given information and apply the relevant relation.

steps.title: This The spatial layout needed to set up

solution.trap: trapezium problem requires the diagram to establish which angles and sides relate PS to QR via the given angle P=30^\circ and tan(∠RSQ)=5/8.

question: A cyclist rides along a track to the top of a hill then immediately turns around and descends along the same track to her starting point. She takes 40 minutes at an average speed of 12 kmh⁻¹ to reach the top. Her average speed for the whole journey is 15 kmh⁻¹. What is the average speed of her descent?

steps.title: Distance up km so total distance km
steps.content: Distance up $=12\times\frac{40}{60}=8$ km, so total distance $=16$ km.

question: A solid cylinder has radius $r$ cm and height $h$ cm. A cube has side length $3r$ cm. The total surface area of the cylinder is equal to four times the total surface area of the cube. Which of the following is an expression for $h$ in terms of $r$?

steps.title: Given cylinder SA cube SA
steps.content: Given cylinder SA $=4\times$cube SA: $2\pi r^2+2\pi rh=216r^2$.

question: Consider the equation $2x^2+4x+c=0$, where $c$ is a constant. The positive difference between the roots of this equation is $\sqrt{10}$. What is the value of $c$?

steps.title: For the difference between roots is
steps.content: For $ax^2+bx+c=0$, the difference between roots is $\dfrac{\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}{a}$.

subtopic: Changing the subject of a formula
question: The variables $x$ and $y$ are related by the equation $x=5-\dfrac{2y^3+1}{1-2y^3}$. Which of the following is a rearrangement to make $y$ the subject?
